background.name: Adventuring Scholar

default: a set of artisan's tools (one of your choice)

entries: You are often driven by a sense of wonder and a desire to uncover the mysteries of the world. You relish the thrill of exploration and discovery and are constantly seeking new challenges and intellectual puzzles to solve. Despite your scholarly tendencies, you are also adept at combat and survival skills.

entries.name: Feature: Researcher's Eye
entries: Your extensive experience with research and analysis has honed your ability to spot important details and patterns. You have advantage on Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to uncover information or solve puzzles.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am always eager to learn new things.
I am quick to ask questions, and I never shy away from a challenge.
I am always looking for an angle or a loophole.
I am a voracious reader and collector of books.
I am always trying to make connections between seemingly unrelated facts.
I am not afraid to take risks in pursuit of knowledge.
I am unflappable in the face of danger.
I never pass up an opportunity to show off my knowledge.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Discovery. Uncovering new knowledge is the most important thing. (Neutral)
Knowledge. The accumulation of knowledge is essential for progress. (Lawful)
Exploration. There's always something new to see and learn. (Chaotic)
Power. Knowledge is power, and I strive to amass as much of it as I can. (Evil)
Education. It is important to share knowledge and help others learn. (Good)
Logic. Emotions should never cloud one's judgment. (Lawful)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I have a deep connection to a certain institution of learning.
I seek to uncover an ancient mystery.
I owe my life to a mentor who taught me everything I know.
I am driven to prove I am better than my peers.
I want to make a name for myself in the scholarly world.
I am searching for a lost relic of great power.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am easily distracted by new and interesting things.
I am too confident in my own knowledge and abilities.
I am quick to judge those who lack my scholarly aptitude.
I am slow to trust those outside my circle of friends and allies.
I am overly competitive and will do anything to be the best.
I have a deep-seated fear of failure and will go to extreme lengths to avoid it.

background.name: Champion

default: a trophy from a past victory (such as a broken piece of armor or a banner)
a token given to you by a mentor or sponsor

entries: You were born with an exceptional talent for combat, and you've spent your life training and honing your skills in order to become a true master of the art of war. You've fought in countless battles and have won fame and glory with your impressive feats of strength and cunning tactics.

entries.name: Feature: Master of Combat
entries: Your skill with weapons and combat techniques is unparalleled. You have advantage on your first attack roll against any creature that has not yet taken a turn in combat. In addition, because of your reputation as a fearsome warrior, you have advantage on any ability check made in a social situation where your reputation carries weight.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I'm always eager for a good fight, no matter the odds.
I have a quick temper, and I'm not afraid to use it.
I enjoy showing off my skills to impress others.
I'm fiercely competitive, and I hate to lose.
I'm loyal to my tribe, and I will do whatever it takes to protect them.
I'm slow to trust outsiders, but I'm always willing to give them a chance.
I'm passionate about my beliefs, and I can be quite persuasive when defending them.
I'm a natural leader, and I take my responsibilities seriously.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Glory. I want to be remembered as one of the greatest warriors of all time. (Any)
Honor. I fight with honor and expect others to do the same. (Good)
Courage. I never back down from a challenge, no matter how dangerous. (Neutral)
Justice. I will do whatever it takes to protect the innocent and defend the weak. (Lawful)
Loyalty. I am devoted to my tribe and will do whatever I can to ensure their safety and prosperity. (Good)
Strength. I respect those who are strong and powerful, and I strive to become even stronger myself. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I have a mentor who taught me everything I know about combat, and I'm fiercely loyal to that person.
I have a close friend or family member who is also a skilled fighter, and we have a friendly rivalry.
I have a patron who sponsors me in battles and expects me to perform well.
I am determined to uphold the honor of my tribe, and I won't let anyone dishonor its legacy.
I have sworn an oath to protect my people and defend our land from harm.
I seek to prove my worthiness and become the greatest warrior my tribe has ever known.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I tend to be overconfident and underestimate my opponents.
I am often dismissive of those who are not skilled fighters.
I have a weakness for gambling and often make foolish bets on the outcome of battles.
I have a short temper, and I have difficulty controlling my emotions in combat.
I am easily influenced by those with more power and status than I have.
I am eager to engage in combat and often rush into battle without considering the consequences.

background.name: Guide

default: a map of a local area
a collection of local stories and legends

entries: You are an expert in navigating the wilderness, whether tracking, mapping, or using some other means. You either work for hire as a guide or volunteer to help others navigate dangerous and unknown terrain.

entries.name: Feature: Expert Guide
entries: As a master of the wilderness, you can navigate even the most difficult terrain and find hidden paths that others might miss. Whenever you lead a group through the wilderness, add your proficiency bonus to the passive Wisdom (Perception) score of each other member of the group. Additionally, you can use your knowledge of the local area to provide information about landmarks, hazards, and resources.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: My approach to navigation and survival is patient and methodical.
I am always on the lookout for new routes and shortcuts through the wilderness.
I am confident that I can lead others through even the most challenging terrain.
I am quiet and observant, and I always scour the natural world for signs and clues.
I am friendly, outgoing, and quick to make friends and allies.
I am competitive, driven, and always eager for a new challenge.
I am loyal and protective and will put myself in danger to protect others.
I am stoic and unemotional, and I rarely show my true feelings.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Discovery. It is my duty to explore new places and uncover new secrets. (Lawful)
Adventure. Life is an adventure, and I'm here to go on it. (Chaotic)
Protection. I must protect the wilderness and those who travel through it. (Good)
Leadership. I must lead others through the wilderness, regardless of the consequences. (Lawful)
Exploration. I must explore and document the world around me. (Neutral)
Survival. It is my duty to ensure the survival of my group, no matter the cost. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I have a beloved pet that I take with me wherever I go.
I owe my life to a stranger who saved me during a journey.
I have a deep connection to a particular place in the wilderness.
I am dedicated to preserving the beauty of the natural world.
I have a close friend who is always by my side on my journeys.
I have a burning desire to prove myself worthy of my guide's mantle.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: My overconfidence in my abilities can make me reckless.
I am often so focused on reaching my destination that I ignore warnings or advice.
I am quick to judge others and slow to listen to their opinions.
I am overly competitive and often push myself and others too hard.
I am easily distracted by the beauty of the natural world.
I am quick to anger when I feel threatened or disrespected.

background.name: Hunter-Gatherer

default: a collection of stories and legends about wild animals and plants

entries: You have spent your life living off the land, hunting game, and gathering food and resources from the wilderness. You have developed a keen eye and ear for tracking and hunting, as well as a deep respect for the natural world.

entries.name: Feature: Nature's Bounty
entries: You are skilled at finding food and resources in the wilderness. If you spend at least 1 hour foraging in a natural environment, you can find enough food and water to sustain yourself and up to five other creatures for 1 day. Additionally, you can identify any plants or animals you encounter and can determine whether they are safe to eat or handle.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I constantly scan my surroundings for signs of danger or opportunity.
I am patient and methodical when tracking prey, and I wait for the perfect moment to strike.
I adapt well to changing circumstances and environments and can survive in a variety of wilderness settings.
I am fiercely independent and would rather rely on my own skills and resources than seek help from others.
I am a natural hunter who can find food and shelter in even the harshest conditions.
I value the natural world and its inhabitants, and I view them not just as resources, but as fellow living creatures.
I have a deep reverence for the spirit of the hunt and take great joy in the challenge of the chase.
I am a survivor who can endure the toughest conditions and come out on the other side.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Survival. The most important thing is staying alive, no matter the cost. (Neutral)
Community. We are all part of the same great circle of life and must work together to ensure its continuation. (Good)
Balance. The balance of the natural world must be maintained, and I must do my part to ensure that this harmony is preserved. (Lawful)
Strength. I must prove myself to be the strongest and most capable of my people so that they know they can depend on me. (Any)
Greed. I must acquire as much wealth and resources as possible and use them to my own advantage. (Evil)
Knowledge. I must seek out the secrets of nature and learn to use them as best I can. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I will protect my family and friends at all costs.
I owe my tribe a great debt I can never repay.
I am determined to prove myself to be the best hunter in the land.
I strive to discover and protect sites of great abundance.
I seek to acquire knowledge and power from the natural world.
I hunt for food not just for myself, but for my entire tribe.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am too trusting of strangers and often put myself in danger.
I am too reckless in the pursuit of my goals and end up taking unnecessary risks.
I am quick to anger in the face of danger or injustice.
I am easily distracted by the sights and sounds of the wild.
My overconfidence in my own abilities leads me to take on extremely difficult tasks.
I am often unwilling to share my resources, even with those who may need them.

background.name: Outcast

default: a musical instrument (one of your choice)
a set of artisan's tools (one of your choice)
a small bag of trinkets and mementos from your tribe

entries: You have been cast out from your community, tribe, or society. Perhaps you were accused of a crime you did not commit or violated a cultural taboo or law. Regardless of the reason, you've learned to survive on your own in an often hostile and unforgiving world.

entries.name: Feature: Outcast's Resilience
entries: You've learned to survive on your own in harsh and unforgiving environments. You are adept at finding shelter, hunting for food, and avoiding danger. You have advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to forage for food, track prey, and find suitable shelter in the wilds.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am fiercely independent and prefer to rely on myself rather than others.
I take great pride in my tribe's heritage and traditions.
I am slow to trust others because I don't want to be betrayed again.
I am quick to anger and act recklessly when my pride is threatened.
I am fiercely loyal to those I trust.
I am wary of strangers but will extend a helping hand if necessary.
I am a bit of a loner, but I still enjoy the company of close friends.
I am haunted by my past, and I strive to make amends for my mistakes.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Honor. No matter how low I have fallen, I will do whatever it takes to restore my honor. (Lawful)
Survival. I protect myself and those I care about, no matter the cost. (Neutral)
Freedom. I won't be tied down by anyone or anything. (Chaotic)
Community. I strive to protect and preserve any community I find myself in. (Lawful)
Vengeance. I seek revenge on those who have wronged me. (Evil)
Redemption. I will make amends for my past mistakes and prove myself worthy. (Good)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I will do anything to protect my former tribe and those I left behind.
I owe a debt of gratitude to those who have helped me on my journey.
I will never forget the person who drove me from my home.
I am determined to clear my name and restore my honor.
I will take any opportunity to make a new home for myself and my loved ones.
I am committed to helping those in need, no matter the danger.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I have a hard time controlling my temper and often act before I think.
I am quick to judge and slow to forgive, even for minor offenses.
I have a hard time trusting others and often assume the worst of people.
I will go to extremes to get revenge on those who have wronged me.
I am overly proud and will not accept help from others.
I am haunted by my past and often feel guilty for my mistakes.

background.name: Salvager

default: a set of artisan's tools (one of your choice)
a small collection of salvaged items (gears, screws, springs, and so on)

entries: You are skilled in the art of salvaging material from junkyards, battlefields, and other abandoned places. You have spent years perfecting your craft, and you have a knack for repairing and repurposing discarded items in order to sell them to the highest bidder.

entries.name: Feature: Salvage Hunter
entries: Years of experience have given you a keen eye for spotting valuable items among piles of junk. When you enter a new area, you can quickly assess the value of any discarded items you come across and can determine which items are worth keeping and which aren't. Additionally, you have a network of contacts in the world of salvage, and you can use your knowledge to track down specific items or to connect with potential buyers.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I'm always looking for a hidden gem among the junk.
I'm always tinkering with something, even when I don't need to.
I'm a scavenger at heart, and I'm not afraid of a little digging.
I'm always looking for ways to improve my craft.
I'm patient and methodical in my work.
I'm a creature of habit who prefers to follow a set routine.
I like to keep my salvage to myself until the right buyer comes along.
I'm quick to jump on any opportunity to make a profit.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Innovation. I believe that anything can be salvaged and put to use with enough creativity and effort. (Lawful)
Profit. I'm always looking for a way to make a quick buck. (Neutral)
Exploration. I'm driven by my curiosity to explore the unknown. (Chaotic)
Preservation. I believe that we must preserve the past in order to move forward. (Good)
Greed. I'm not above taking advantage of someone's misfortune in order to get ahead. (Evil)
Resourcefulness. I'm used to making do with whatever I can find. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I owe my success to a mentor who taught me the ins and outs of salvaging.
I am always on the lookout for my next big score—the one that will set me up for life.
I'm searching for a rare item that I've been told will make me wealthy.
I'm on a mission to recover a lost family heirloom.
I have a rival who is always trying to one-up me.
I have a special bond with a particular item I've salvaged.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am always on the lookout for new salvage opportunities, even in the midst of danger.
I am always tinkering with something, even if it doesn't need fixing.
I hoard my salvage, even if it means denying others what they need.
I am never satisfied with my work and am always striving to improve.
I'm easily distracted by shiny objects.
I am paranoid about losing my salvage and will go to great lengths to protect it, even if that means hurting others.

background.name: Spiritualist

default: a spirit token (such as a carved talisman or a vial of consecrated water)
a personal relic (such as a piece of jewelry or a woven bracelet)

entries: You have always had a deep connection to the spirit world, and you have spent your life studying and practicing the art of communing with spirits. You can sense the presence of supernatural entities and communicate with them, and you frequently seek their wisdom and guidance.

entries.name: Feature: Spirit Guide
entries: You have a spirit guide who assists you in your spiritual work. This guide can not only provide you with insight and guidance, but also warn you of danger or threats. As an action, you can communicate with your spirit guide, who might be able to grant you information related to your current situation or quest. The GM determines what information your spirit guide offers you and how that information is conveyed to you. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus and regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am a patient and thoughtful listener who is attentive to the whispers of the spirits.
I am deeply empathetic and compassionate, and I strive to help others overcome their spiritual struggles.
I am curious and adventurous and always seek to learn more about the spirit world.
I am introspective and reflective, and I often spend long periods of time in meditation and contemplation.
I am brave and courageous, and I am willing to face the unknown in my search for knowledge and understanding.
I am open-minded and accepting, and I allow new beliefs and experiences to shape my understanding of the spirit world.
I am wise and discerning, and I carefully consider all the advice I receive from spirits and other supernatural entities.
I am determined and resolute, and I never give up on a quest for spiritual knowledge.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Knowledge. It is my duty to seek out the secrets of the spirit world and to share what I learn with others. (Neutral)
Compassion. I strive to alleviate the suffering of spirits and mortals alike. (Good)
Balance. I seek to maintain equilibrium between the spirit world and the material plane. (Lawful)
Discovery. I am constantly pursuing new spiritual knowledge. (Chaotic)
Harmony. All living things are connected in a grand spiritual harmony. (Good)
Mystery. The unknown is a source of wonder and fascination. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I owe a great debt to the spirit that saved my life.
I am devoted to helping others on their spiritual journeys.
I will never forget the wisdom my mentor imparted to me.
I strive to protect the interests of my spiritual family.
I seek to unravel the mysteries of the spirit world.
I am determined to master the secrets of spiritual power.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I put too much trust in spirits and other supernatural entities.
I am easily swayed by the opinions of others.
I am too quick to believe in the impossible.
I rely too much on the guidance of the spirits.
I am too eager to take risks in the pursuit of spiritual knowledge.
I am too slow to accept new ideas or beliefs.

background.name: Swamp Angler

default: a set of artisan's tools (one of your choice)
a small collection of dried fish and other preserved foods

entries: You were born and raised in the murky swamps, where you learned the art of survival through fishing and hunting. As a swamp angler, you know the ins and outs of the swamp's ecosystem, from the best fishing spots to the dangers that lurk beneath the murky waters.

entries.name: Feature: Swampcraft
entries: You have a deep understanding of how to navigate and survive in the swamp. You have advantage on ability checks made to navigate a swamp, find food and potable water in a swamp, and avoid hazards such as quicksand and hostile swamp creatures. You also know how to construct shelters and rafts using natural materials found in swamps.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am always on the lookout for my next meal, no matter where I am.
I am cautious around strangers and always keep my guard up.
I am fiercely independent and prefer to rely on myself rather than others.
I am always looking for ways to improve my fishing and hunting skills.
I have a dry sense of humor that sometimes catches people off guard.
I am a bit of a hoarder, and I have trouble parting with items that I think might be useful.
I am often stubborn and willful, and I refuse to back down even when doing so might be in my best interest.
I am curious about the world beyond the swamp and often find myself daydreaming about far-off places.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Self-Reliance. I am responsible for my own survival, and I will do whatever it takes to stay alive. (Neutral)
Adaptability. Life in the swamp is unpredictable, and I must be ready to adapt to any situation. (Chaotic)
Resilience. Living in the swamp is tough, but I'm tougher. I can handle anything the swamp throws my way. (Any)
Cooperation. I prefer to work alone, but I know I sometimes need help from others to survive. (Lawful)
Freedom. I value my independence and will not be controlled by anyone or anything. (Chaotic)
Exploration. The world is vast and full of wonders, and I want to experience as much of it as I can. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I am fiercely protective of my family and will do anything to keep them safe.
I owe a debt to a fellow swamp angler who saved my life.
I have a deep connection to the swamp and feel a sense of duty to protect it from those who would harm it.
I have a prized catch that got off the hook. I'm going to catch it again one day, and this time, it won't be so lucky.
I am searching for a lost artifact that is rumored to be hidden somewhere in the swamp.
I have a secret that could destroy my reputation if it were ever revealed.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am so shortsighted that I focus too much on the present and end up ignoring the long-term consequences of my actions.
I take risks that others might consider foolish or dangerous.
I am paranoid and suspicious of others, even when there's no reason to be.
I have a deep-seated fear of leaving the swamp and become agitated or panicked when forced to travel beyond its borders.
I exaggerate my accomplishments and skills, which can get me into trouble when I can't back up my claims.
I hold grudges against those who wrong me, and I seek revenge even when it is not justified.

background.name: Swamp Chef

default: a recipe book containing your favorite swamp dishes
a selection of herbs and spices

entries: You have spent your life living off the land in the swamps and marshes of western Immoren. Along the way, you've learned how to cook up delicious and unique meals made from local flora and fauna, no matter how humble or unappetizing the ingredients.

entries.name: Feature: Swamp Cuisine
entries: You are a master of swamp cuisine, and your cooking skills are renowned throughout the region. As a part of a short rest you can prepare a meal that feeds up to 6 creatures. Each creature that consumes a meal you prepared regains a number of hit points equal to your proficiency bonus. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am always happy to share my food and my knowledge with others.
I am resourceful and inventive, and I can create a meal out of almost anything.
I am outgoing and talkative, and I love to swap stories and recipes with others.
I am fiercely independent, and I prefer to rely on myself rather than others.
I am patient and calm, even in the face of adversity.
I am passionate and enthusiastic about cooking and the outdoors.
I am observant and always looking for new ingredients and ideas.
I am generous and always willing to help out those in need.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Generosity. Sharing is the greatest joy. (Good)
Adaptability. No matter the challenge, I can find a way. (Chaotic)
Independence. Relying on myself is the only way. (Neutral)
Exploration. There is always something new to discover. (Chaotic)
Creativity. Innovation is the key to success. (Any)
Resourcefulness. Nothing is ever truly wasted. (Any)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I owe a great debt to the person who taught me to cook.
I am loyal to my family and always put them first.
I have a deep connection with the swamp that I call home.
I have made a vow to cook with only the rarest ingredients.
I strive to create the most delicious dishes possible.
I will do anything to ensure that my recipes are passed down to future generations.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am overly competitive with others, especially when it comes to cooking.
I am easily distracted by shiny objects and new ingredients.
I have a hard time trusting people I don't know.
I take risks without considering the consequences.
I am slow to forgive those who wrong me.
I can't say no to a good meal.

background.name: Tribal Councilor

default: a symbol of your tribal office (such as a necklace or medallion)
a tribal artifact (such as a carved totem or woven basket)
a token of appreciation from a fellow council member or tribal leader

entries: Born and raised in a close-knit community that values tradition and the wisdom of its elders, you were chosen by your tribe to serve on its council. You are respected for your knowledge of tribal law, your ability to negotiate with other groups, and your dedication to the well-being of your people.

entries.name: Feature: Wisdom of the Elders
entries: You have a deep understanding of your tribe's customs and beliefs, as well as a keen insight into the personalities and motivations of its members. You have advantage on Wisdom (Insight) checks related to members of your tribe, and you have a network of contacts throughout the region who can provide you with information and assistance (at the GM's discretion).

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am a patient and understanding listener, and I value the opinions of others.
I have a talent for mediating and resolving conflict, and I strive to find peaceful solutions to disputes.
I am deeply loyal to my tribe and will do whatever is necessary to protect its interests.
I am a wise and respected leader, but I am also humble and willing to learn from others.
I am brave and courageous in the face of adversity, and I will not back down from a challenge.
I am passionate about justice and fairness.
I am generous and compassionate toward my fellow tribe members, and I strive to create a safe and supportive environment for them.
I am determined to achieve my goals, and I will not give up until I have succeeded.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Unity. We are stronger together than we are on our own. (Lawful)
Community. The well-being of my tribe is my highest priority. (Good)
Honor. I will uphold the traditions and values of my people. (Lawful)
Growth. I will use my knowledge and experience to help my tribe thrive. (Neutral)
Exploration. I will seek out new paths and possibilities for my people. (Chaotic)
Justice. I will ensure that all are treated fairly and with respect. (Good)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I am fiercely loyal to my tribe and will do whatever it takes to protect them.
I have a deep connection to the natural world, and I strive to protect and preserve it.
I have a strong sense of responsibility to my family and ancestors.
I am committed to helping my tribe members, no matter the cost.
I owe a debt of gratitude to those who have helped me in the past.
I have a personal mission to expand my people's knowledge and understanding.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am overly trusting of strangers and too quick to take them at their word.
I am stubborn and have a hard time admitting when I am wrong.
I am too proud and have a hard time asking for help when I need it.
I am often swayed by emotional appeals and can be easily manipulated.
I am fervently loyal to my tribe, to the exclusion of other considerations.
I am too quick to jump to conclusions and often make rash decisions.

background.name: Witch Doctor

default: a set of common clothes (customary for your tribe)

entries: You are a practitioner of ancient, primal magic, and you draw upon the spirits of the earth, the sky, and your ancestors to heal the sick, divine the future, and protect your tribe. Your mysterious powers are often misunderstood by outsiders, but you have spent your life honing your craft and developing a deep understanding of the natural world.

entries.name: Feature: Spiritual Healer
entries: You can use your knowledge of herbs and magic to heal injuries and cure diseases. If you spend 10 minutes treating a wounded creature, it regains a number of hit points equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1). You can use this ability a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.caption: Personality Trait
rows: I am a deeply spiritual person who always tries to understand and connect with the spirits of the natural world.
I am knowledgeable and wise, and I have a deep understanding of the natural world and its many secrets.
I am patient and methodical, and I can wait for the right moment to act or make a decision.
I am fiercely loyal to my tribe and will do whatever it takes to protect them from harm.
I am driven by a desire to help and heal others, no matter the cost.
I am a natural leader who can inspire and motivate others with my words and actions.
I am bold and daring, and I am not afraid to take risks or stand up to those who threaten my people.
I am a master of disguise who can blend in with any crowd or situation.

entries.caption: Ideal
rows: Balance. The natural world is in perfect harmony and must be protected and respected at all costs. (Lawful)
Knowledge. I strive to learn as much as I can about the natural world and its many mysteries. (Neutral)
Power. I use my power for the benefit of my people and to protect them from harm. (Lawful)
Growth. I use my knowledge and power to help my people grow and prosper. (Good)
Freedom. I do what I must to ensure my people's freedom and independence. (Chaotic)
Tradition. I honor and uphold the traditions of my people, no matter the cost. (Lawful)

entries.caption: Bond
rows: I will do whatever it takes to protect my tribe and its secrets.
I am devoted to helping and healing those in need.
I will seek revenge against those who have wronged my people.
I seek to obtain powerful artifacts that will benefit my people.
I strive to maintain balance and harmony in the natural world.
I will find a way to restore the ancient traditions of my people.

entries.caption: Flaw
rows: I am too trusting of others and can be easily manipulated.
I am overly protective of my people and their secrets.
I sometimes forget to consider the consequences of my actions.
I am quick to judge others and overlook important details.
I often make rash, impulsive decisions.
I let my emotions get the better of me and am easily angered.

background.name: Irregulars
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} Not every mercenary company in the Iron Kingdoms operates with the benefit and legitimacy of a charter. Your group might be a former military unit, charter-less mercenaries, or simply warriors who fight for themselves and those they protect.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to an Irregulars company.

entries.name: Downtime: Sell-Swords
entries: Irregulars have a reputation for being hardy warriors who are always ready for a fight. Members of your company can spend their downtime earning a little extra coin by hiring themselves out as bodyguards, sentries, or even gladiators. For each day that you spend in this fashion, you earn {@dice 1d6} gp, but on a roll of 1, your hit point maximum decreases by 1d6 until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Your company's prestige increases through acts such as: killing or capturing an enemy commander; routing a rival mercenary company; negotiating a contract with a warlord, noble, or military commander; saving the life of a chieftain or notable figure; capturing a warjack, warbeast, or valuable possession from a rival company.

entries.name: Quirks
entries: Many adventuring companies have quirks that set them apart. Choose a quirk or roll on the table: (1) Former irregulars unit from a nation's military; (2) Original members were deserters or criminals; (3) Former tribal warriors who were bought off; (4) Camp-movers who leave no trace behind; (5) Strict self-imposed rules about contract terms; (6) All lost someone in the Claiming; (7) Folk heroes from the same small village; (8) Known for an imposing captured battle standard; (9) New inductees take on part of a fallen founder's name; (10) Company made its name taking the bloodiest, most unwanted jobs.

entries.name: Tier 1 — Backwoods Recruits
entries: {@b Encampment.} Your ragtag group maintains a simple but effective encampment of tents, lean-tos, and natural shelters. You can stay at your encampment and maintain a poor lifestyle at no expense.

{@b Masters of None.} When you make an ability check using a skill or tool you aren't proficient with, you can add your proficiency bonus to the roll. You can use this benefit once between long rests.

{@b No Nonsense.} You can replace one proficiency gained from your background or class with proficiency in the Intimidation skill, and you add double your proficiency bonus when using Intimidation to negotiate or collect on a mercenary contract.

entries.name: Tier 2 — Ragged Soldiers
entries: {@b Ambuscade.} During the first round of combat, you have advantage on attack rolls against creatures that haven't taken an action yet that turn.
{@b Fresh Faces.} Your growing reputation attracts new recruits. You gain {@dice 1d6} hirelings with a challenge rating of 1/2 or lower.
{@b Pay Up.} When you spend downtime working as a sell-sword, you gain {@dice 1d10} gp for each day spent doing such work, rather than {@dice 1d6} gp.

entries.name: Tier 3 — Motley Militia
entries: {@b Advance Payment.} When you take on a mercenary contract, you can demand up to 200 gp in advance, depending on the total terms of the contract.
{@b Champions-at-Arms.} You gain your choice of {@dice 1d8} hirelings with a challenge rating of 1 or lower or {@dice 1d4} hirelings with a challenge rating of 2 or lower.

{@b Frontier Fortress.} You've upgraded your encampment to a fortified garrison with barracks, a smithy, and stables that can hold a warjack or warbeast. You can maintain a modest lifestyle at no expense. Creatures that attempt to attack you while you are in your fortress have disadvantage on attack rolls during the first round of combat.

entries.name: Tier 4 — Frontier Legends
entries: {@b Hard-Bitten.} Having stared death in the face on battlefields all over western Immoren, you've become inured to the hardships and dangers of your trade. Members of your company—including your hirelings—have advantage on saving throws to avoid being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.
{@b Fearsome Fighters.} You gain your choice of {@dice 1d10} hirelings with a challenge rating of 2 or lower or a steamjack, myrmidon, or warbeast with a challenge rating of 5 or lower.

background.name: Posse
entries: {@b Adventuring Company.} When the territory of a swampie village or gatorfolk congregation must be defended, they round up the biggest, strongest, and angriest members of their community and form a posse. What posses lack in training or equipment, they make up for in determination.

entries.name: Prerequisites
entries: Any character can belong to a Posse company. Characters with proficiency in the Survival skill are particularly prized, but spellcasters and other classes are also welcome.

entries.name: Special: The Toughest Around
entries: When it comes time to defend your tribal territory, you need the sturdiest folks in town. Each member's hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to twice the member's level. Whenever a member of the posse gains a level, that member's hit point maximum increases by an additional 2 hit points.

entries.name: Company Accomplishments
entries: Your company's prestige increases through acts such as: defending the community from a force with superior numbers; capturing or slaying a large beast causing trouble in the region; bringing in a local criminal or proving someone is wrongfully accused; protecting the community during a disaster or famine; expanding the territory or resources available to the community.

entries.name: Tier 1 — Unruly Mob
entries: {@b Bring 'Em Back Alive.} You can replace one proficiency you gained from your background or class with proficiency in the Survival skill, and you can double your proficiency bonus when you use the Survival skill for tracking.

{@b No Place Like Home.} So long as you are in your community, you can enjoy a modest lifestyle at no expense. The community will protect you from discovery and provide wares and services at little or no charge if they can.

entries.name: Tier 2 — Local Toughs
entries: {@b Billet.} The community has set aside a structure for your use—a converted barn, empty house, or storefront—that houses your company, provides room for extra supplies, and can act as a makeshift jail.
{@b The Law in These Parts.} You are seen as arbiters of local justice. Those in your area will generally go along with you as you interrogate people, search houses, take captives, and so on.

{@b Spiritual Support.} Your community's spiritual advisors have taken a keen interest in your well-being. You gain one bone grinder, cleric, shaman, or other spiritual leader with a challenge rating of 3 or lower.

entries.name: Tier 3 — Regulators
entries: {@b Deputize.} When you need them, the people of your community will take up arms to help repel an attack or bring down a threat. At the GM's discretion, you can unofficially deputize up to {@dice 3d6} members of your community with a challenge rating of 1/4 or lower, plus one steamjack, myrmidon, warbeast, or community member with a challenge rating of 6 or lower. These individuals follow your orders for 48 hours or until the specific problem is resolved. Once you deputize members in this way, you can't do so again for {@dice 1d4} days.

{@b Friends in High Places.} You have the ear of whoever is in charge in your community. You are largely immune to being held accountable for your actions if you can demonstrate they are in the best interests of the community. Even if you get into trouble outside your territory, your contacts may send someone to advocate on your behalf or break you out of jail.

entries.name: Tier 4 — Community Champions
entries: {@b Hard-Won Respect.} Brigands and raiders for leagues around know better than to bring trouble to your door, and the people of your community see you as heroes. Local leaders must actively curry your favor to maintain their position of power, since anyone you support is sure to have a significant advantage.

trait.name: Applied Knowledge
entries: When the scholar hits a creature of type aberration, beast, dragon, fey, giant, monstrosity, ooze, or undead with a weapon attack, it can add its Intelligence modifier to the damage roll.

trait.name: Keen Hearing and Sight
entries: The scholar has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on hearing or sight.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The scholar makes two shortsword attacks.

action.name: Shortsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d6+2} piercing damage.

action.name: Repeating Rifle
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 4} to hit, range 60/180 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 2d8+2} piercing damage.

reaction.name: Duck!
entries: When the scholar is targeted by a ranged attack, it can drop prone. If it does so, it has advantage on Dexterity saving throws until the start of its next turn.

trait.name: Favored Enemy
entries: The slayer has advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track beasts, and on Intelligence checks to recall information about them.

trait.name: Prey
entries: As a bonus action, the slayer marks a creature it can see within 90 feet as its prey. The first time each turn that the slayer hits the prey with a weapon attack, it deals an extra 1d6 damage. The mark lasts for 1 hour or until the slayer marks a new target.

trait.name: Relentless Pursuit
entries: The slayer ignores difficult terrain when moving toward a creature it has marked as its prey.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The slayer makes two longsword attacks.

action.name: Longsword
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d8+2} slashing damage, or {@damage 1d10+2} slashing damage if used with two hands.

action.name: Hunting Rifle
entries: {@atk rw} {@hit 5} to hit, range 80/240 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 2d10+3} piercing damage.

reaction.name: Quick Reflexes
entries: When a beast hits the slayer with a melee attack, the slayer can make one melee weapon attack against that beast.

headerEntries: The stoneshaper is a 6th-level spellcaster. Its spellcasting ability is Wisdom (spell save DC 14, {@hit 6} to hit with spell attacks). It has the following spells prepared:

action.name: Unarmed Strike
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 4} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d4+1} bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Earth's Power (1/Day)
entries: The stoneshaper causes the ground in a 20-foot radius centered on a point it can see within 60 feet to become difficult terrain for 1 minute.

action.name: Quiet the Stones (1/Day)
entries: The stoneshaper silences all mechanikal and magical sounds within 30 feet of it for 1 minute.

action.name: Repair (1/Day)
entries: The stoneshaper touches a construct or mechanikal device and restores {@dice 1d6} hit points to it.

action.name: Stone Spray (Recharge 5–6)
entries: {@atk rs} {@hit 6} to hit. Each creature in a 30-foot cone must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d8} bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

spellcasting.name: Spellcasting

trait.name: Camouflage
entries: The stoneward has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in natural terrain.

trait.name: Pathfinder
entries: Difficult terrain composed of natural elements doesn't cost the stoneward extra movement.

trait.name: Self-Sacrifice
entries: When an ally within 5 feet of the stoneward would take damage, the stoneward can choose to take that damage instead.

trait.name: Service Beyond Death
entries: When the stoneward is reduced to 0 hit points, it can use its reaction to cast a spell it knows that has a casting time of 1 action.

trait.name: Stone Guide
entries: The stoneward can communicate with stone and earth, learning general information about the area within 1 mile.

action.name: Voulge
entries: {@atk mw} {@hit 3} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d6+1} bludgeoning damage, or {@damage 1d8+1} bludgeoning damage if used with two hands. This is a magical attack.

entries: Skilled tacticians and natural leaders in war, warlords' very presence on the battlefield is enough to inspire their allies and intimidate their foes.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you have significant experience studying, tracking, hunting, and even talking to a certain type of enemy.

Choose a type of favored prey: beasts, constructs, dragonspawn, drakes, elementals, fey (grymkin), fiends (infernals), monstrosities, plants, trolls, or undead. Alternatively, you can select two races of humanoid (such as farrow and trollkin) as favored prey.

Once per turn, when you hit a favored prey with a weapon or spell attack, you can add a d4 to your damage roll. This die changes as you gain monster hunter levels, as shown in the Prey column of the Monster Hunter table.
Additionally, you may also add your prey die on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track your favored prey, as well as on Intelligence checks to recall information about them.
When you gain this feature, you also learn one language of your choice that is spoken by your favored prey, if they speak one at all.

You choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language, at 6th, 9th, and 14th level. At these levels, you may also change an earlier favored prey choice, but you do not learn a new language or forget the older one.

classFeature.name: Weapon Expertise
entries: Beginning at 1st level, you have learned to apply your monster hunting skills when fighting with your preferred weaponry. When you have advantage on an attack roll against one of your favored enemies using a weapon you're proficient with, you can reroll one of the dice once.

classFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 2nd level, you adopt a particular style of fighting as your specialty. Choose one of the following options. You can't take a Fighting Style option more than once, even if you later get to choose again. You can choose an additional fighting style at 13th level.

entries.name: Defense
entries: While you are wearing armor, you gain a +1 bonus to AC.

entries.name: Great Weapon Fighting
entries: When you roll a 1 or 2 on a damage die for an attack you make with a melee weapon that you are wielding with two hands, you can reroll the die and must use the new roll, even if the new roll is a 1 or a 2. The weapon must have the two-handed or versatile property for you to gain this benefit.

entries.name: Keen Shot
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to damage rolls you make with two-handed ranged weapons.

entries.name: Sharpshooter
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack rolls you make with firearms.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn. The number of attacks increases to three when you reach 17th level in this class.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey Improvement
entries: At 6th level, you choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language. You may also change an earlier favored prey choice.

classFeature.name: Survivalist
entries: At 6th level, your survival instincts have been sharpened by all the monsters you've hunted. When you roll a 9 or lower on an Intelligence (Investigation) check to investigate an area your favored enemies have recently been to or currently live in, or on a Wisdom (Survival) check to track your favored enemies, you can treat the d20 roll as a 10.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey Improvement
entries: At 9th level, you choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language. You may also change an earlier favored prey choice.

classFeature.name: Grievous Wounds
entries: At 10th level, fighting monsters has given you a unique insight into their physiology, allowing you to see their weak points and understand how to inflict the most damage upon them. Your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19–20 against your favored enemies. When you score a critical hit against one of your favored enemies, the target can't regain hit points until the start of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Additional Fighting Style
entries: At 13th level, you can choose one additional fighting style from the Fighting Style feature options.

classFeature.name: Favored Prey Improvement
entries: At 14th level, you choose one additional favored prey, as well as an associated language. You may also change an earlier favored prey choice.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack (2)
entries: Beginning at 17th level, you can attack three times whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeature.name: To the Ends of Caen
entries: At 18th level, you are tireless in pursuit of your prey. You gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws, and you have advantage on saving throws when failure would result in gaining one or more levels of exhaustion.

classFeature.name: Apex Predator
entries: At 20th level, attacks on your favored prey leave them rattled and unable to respond. When you hit your favored prey with a weapon attack, you can forgo adding your prey die to the damage roll to incapacitate your target. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + proficiency bonus + Dexterity modifier) or be stunned until the end of your next turn. Doing this constitutes your use of the prey die for your turn. Additionally, you are immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.

classFeature.name: Command Range
entries: At 1st level, your inspiring presence can steel your fellow warriors' nerves and even rally them before their panic becomes a full-blown rout. You have a command range for your warlord abilities. Your warlord level determines the distance of your command range, as shown in the Command Range column of the Warlord table.

classFeature.name: Battle Command
entries: Also at 1st level, you can use a bonus action to issue a command to a friendly creature you can see within your command range. The creature can use its reaction to make a weapon attack or to take the Dash, Disengage, or Help action on its turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Inspiring Presence
entries: At 1st level, you can use a bonus action to choose a number of creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one creature) within your command range. Each target that can see or hear you gains temporary hit points equal to your warlord level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Tactical Strike
entries: At 2nd level, when you use your action to make a weapon attack against a creature you can see that is within 5 feet of a friendly creature you can see, you add your Charisma modifier to the damage roll.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, and again at 8th, 12th, 16th, and 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature. If your campaign uses the optional feats rule, you can forgo taking this feature to take a feat of your choice instead.

classFeature.name: Extra Attack
entries: Beginning at 5th level, you can attack twice, instead of once, whenever you take the Attack action on your turn.

classFeature.name: Warlord's Orders
entries: Also at 5th level, you master the art of barking out orders in the chaos of battle. As an action, you can issue a command to nearby allies. Choose a number of friendly creatures up to your proficiency bonus that are within your command range and that can hear and understand you. Each chosen creature can use its reaction to move up to its speed or to make a weapon attack.

classFeature.name: Inspiring Leadership
entries: At 6th level, when you use your Inspiring Presence, affected creatures have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened} while they have temporary hit points granted by Inspiring Presence.

classFeature.name: Warlord's Tactics
entries: At 9th level, you can use a bonus action to choose a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. Until the end of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within your command range have advantage on attack rolls against the chosen creature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Overwhelming Force
entries: At 11th level, when you hit a creature with an attack on your turn, a friendly creature within your command range can use its reaction to make a melee weapon attack. If that attack hits, the target is knocked prone but doesn't take damage.

classFeature.name: Coordinated Assault
entries: At 13th level, when you use your Warlord's Orders, each affected creature that chooses to make a weapon attack deals an extra 1d8 weapon damage when it hits a target. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Master Tactician
entries: Beginning at 15th level, you can take an additional action on your turn. This action can be used only to take the Attack action or to use your Battle Command. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

classFeature.name: Supreme Commander
entries: When you reach 17th level, you become a master of leadership and tactics, and you can inspire your allies and dominate your enemies with ease.
You can use your Inspiring Presence a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

When you use your Tactical Strike, you can choose up to three creatures within your command range. Each target must make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn.

classFeature.name: Unbreakable Resolve
entries: At 18th level, you and friendly creatures within your command range can't be {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

classFeature.name: Battlefield Champion
entries: At 20th level, you embody raw power and command. Your Strength and Charisma scores increase by 4. Your maximum for those scores is now 24.

subclass.name: Big-Game Hunter
subclassFeatures: The Bigger They Are|Monster Hunter|IRKDw|Big-Game Hunter|IRKDw|11

subclass.name: Wolf of Orboros
subclassFeatures: Hungry like the Wolf|Fighter|PHB|Wolf of Orboros|IRKDw|10

subclass.name: Twin Soul
subclassFeatures: Two Heads Are Better than One|Sorcerer|PHB|Twin Soul|IRKDw|14

subclass.name: Mist Speaker
subclassFeatures: Shamanic Rites: Dreams of Ashiga|Shaman|IRKBnB|Mist Speaker|IRKDw|2

entries.name: Steady Aim
entries: At 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you learn to make stunning long-distance shots. If you forgo moving during your turn and make a ranged attack with a rifle at long range, attacking at long range doesn't impose disadvantage.

entries.name: Fire and Fade
entries: Also at 3rd level, you become adept at sniping enemies and disappearing into nearby cover before you can be spotted. When you hit a creature with ranged weapon attack, you can use your reaction to take the Hide action.

subclassFeature.name: Run and Gun
entries: At 7th level, you have mastered the ability to use your ranged attacks to cover your next move. If you hit a creature with a ranged attack, you can move up to your speed as a bonus action. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: The Bigger They Are
entries: At 11th level, your knowledge of the hunt allows you to take down larger monsters more effectively. When you hit a creature that is at least one size larger than you, you can deal extra weapon damage equal to your proficiency bonus. (Double this bonus if you scored a critical hit.) You can use this feature three times. You regain expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Keen Eye
entries: Starting at 15th level, you are adept at avoiding the teeth and claws of large beasts. Whenever a creature a size one size or more larger than you makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction to impose disadvantage on it. You must use this feature before you know the outcome of the attack roll. You may use this feature three times, and regain all uses after a short or long rest.

entries.name: Guerrilla Tactics
entries: At 3rd level, your ability to sneak away and hide from your enemies becomes uncanny. You can take a bonus action on each of your turns to take the Hide action.

entries.name: Ambush
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you're deadliest when you can surprise your foes. You may add your proficiency bonus to initiative rolls. Additionally, in the first round of combat you may add a roll of your prey die to damage against each creature you hit with a weapon attack who has not yet acted, even if that creature is not your favored enemy.

subclassFeature.name: Predatory Drive
entries: Starting at 7th level, while tracking, you and your group may move stealthily at a normal pace, and remain alert to danger while you do so. You also have advantage on saving throws to avoid exhaustion. If tracking your favored prey, you may also add your prey die to the roll. Additionally, you may remove a level of exhaustion after you take a short rest, and regain the ability to do so once you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Treewalker
entries: At 11th level, you gain a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. Additionally, moving through nonmagical difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement.

subclassFeature.name: Master Tracker
entries: At 15th level, your senses are attuned to tracking prey and are equal to those of the most cunning predators. When you succeed on a Wisdom (Survival) check to locate your favored enemies, you know their location if it is within 100 feet.

entries.name: Intimidating Presence
entries: At 3rd level, when you use your Inspiring Presence, you also frighten nearby enemies. A hostile creature of your choice within 5 feet of you that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the creature becomes {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. Attacks against the frightened creature deal extra weapon damage equal to your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Brutal Tactics
entries: Beginning at 7th level, when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can force the creature to make a Strength saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the creature is knocked prone, and you can move up to half your speed without provoking opportunity attacks from the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Conqueror's Presence
entries: At 10th level, you can use your action to gain temporary hit points equal to your warlord level + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). In addition, you and friendly creatures within your command range have advantage on weapon attack rolls until the end of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Bloodthirsty Orders
entries: At 14th level, when you use your Battle Command, choose a friendly creature within your command range. That creature can immediately take the Attack action, and its next attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

entries.name: Defensive Maneuvers
entries: At 3rd level, when you use your Battle Command, you can choose one creature within your command range. The target has resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Sentinel's Stance
entries: At 7th level, you can use your action to enter a sentinel's stance. While in this stance, you have advantage on opportunity attacks, and creatures provoke opportunity attacks from you even if they take the Disengage action before leaving your reach. Additionally, you can use your reaction to make a weapon attack against a creature that attacks a friendly creature within 5 feet of you, provided that you can see the attacker.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian's Eye
entries: When you reach 10th level, you learn how to keep an eye on everyone under your command. When a friendly creature within your command range makes a saving throw, you can use your reaction to shout a warning. If the creature can hear you, it has advantage on the saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Indomitable Will
entries: At 14th level, while you are conscious, friendly creatures within your command range can't be {@condition frightened} or {@condition stunned}.

entries.name: Tactical Analysis
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can use an action to choose a creature that you can see within your command range. You learn the creature's current hit points; any damage immunities, resistances, or vulnerabilities it has; and any conditions affecting it. Additionally, the next time you or a friendly creature attacks the target creature before the end of your next turn, the attacker has advantage on the attack roll.

subclassFeature.name: Controlled Advance
entries: At 7th level, you can use your action to direct your allies' advance with precision. Each friendly creature within your command range can move up to its speed and has resistance to all damage from opportunity attacks. Additionally, until the end of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within your command range have advantage on attack rolls made against any creature that made an opportunity attack against a friendly creature during this movement. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Strategist
entries: Starting at 10th level, when you roll initiative, you can take a bonus action to grant yourself and friendly creatures within your command range a bonus to their initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). Additionally, when you use your Inspiring Presence, each affected creature gains a bonus to attack rolls equal to your Charisma modifier while it has temporary hit points granted by Inspiring Presence.

subclassFeature.name: Warlord's Gambit
entries: At 14th level, when you use your Tactical Strike, you can choose to forgo the damage bonus and instead force the target to make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier. On a failed save, the target is {@condition stunned} until the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Field Surgery
entries: Starting at 3rd level when you choose this specialization, you know the anatomy and physiology of living creatures just as well as you know the properties of chemicals and reagents. You gain proficiency with your choice of either a forensic kit or a poisoner's kit, and you double your proficiency bonus when you make Wisdom (Medicine) checks. You also gain a free clockwork injector if you don't already have one.

entries.name: New Fast Brew: Simple Stimulant
entries: You mix ingredients to create a dose of simple stimulant—a bitter, milky fluid that provides a surge of energy and alertness. As an action, a creature can consume a dose of simple stimulant to ignore the highest level of exhaustion it currently suffers. This effect lasts for 1 hour. In addition, a creature can consume a dose of simple stimulant during a short rest to reduce its exhaustion level by 1.

entries.name: This May Sting a Little
entries: At 3rd level, you become adept at using clockwork injectors to efficiently deliver alchemical infusions. As a bonus action, you can make a Wisdom (Medicine) check against a creature that is not a construct or an undead, contested by the creature's Charisma (Deception) check. If you succeed, you have advantage on attack rolls against that creature with your clockwork injector until the start of your next turn. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Medicinal Properties
entries: At 6th level, you have learned how to work quickly and efficiently by piggybacking multiple effects on a single dose. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose one of the following additional effects. These effects don't affect constructs or undead.

entries.name: Adrenaline
entries: Until the start of your next turn, the creature rolls one additional weapon damage die when it hits a target with a melee attack. When this effect wears off, the creature takes 1d10 poison damage.

entries.name: Anesthesia
entries: The creature gains temporary hit points equal to your alchemist level. These temporary hit points last for 10 minutes.

entries.name: Deliriant
entries: The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} of all other creatures for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Inhibitor
entries: The creature must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or be unable to cast spells until the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Revivifier
entries: The creature regains a number of hit points equal to one roll of its Hit Dice. This effect doesn't expend any of the creature's Hit Dice.

entries.name: Sedative
entries: The creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Stabilizer
entries: The creature can end either one disease or one condition afflicting it. The condition can be blinded, charmed, deafened, frightened, paralyzed, poisoned, or stunned. In addition, the creature is immune to the chosen condition until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Animal Magnetism
entries: At 10th level, the often unconventional nature of your experiments forces you to ply your trade on the fringes of civilization, where you must regularly experiment on the beasts of the field. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose to apply the effects of this feature instead of one of the effects provided by your Medicinal Properties.

A creature affected by your Animal Magnetism must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed by you for a number of hours equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1). While charmed in this way, the creature treats you as a friend and obeys any verbal commands that you issue to it (no action required by you), as long as it can understand you. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each hour, or immediately if you or one of your companions harms it. This feature has no effect on constructs or undead.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Adrenalize
entries: Starting at 14th level, you learn how to add a quick jolt to your alchemical concoctions that can help 'juice up' beasts and soldiers on the battlefield. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose two additional effects from your Medicinal Properties, rather than just one. You can use your Animal Magnetism to replace one of these effects.

subclassFeature.name: Life Is Pain
entries: At 18th level, you embrace the unpredictability that comes with experimenting directly on living subjects and are quite comfortable ignoring their safety in order to conduct your unorthodox and sometimes unethical experiments. When a creature is affected by an alchemical item that you created, you can choose to increase or decrease one of that creature's ability scores by an amount equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1) for 1 minute. After this time, the creature's ability score returns to normal, but the creature must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take 1d10 poison damage and become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. The creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you gain proficiency in the Nature and Survival skills, as well as proficiency with a trapper's kit. In addition, you can use your Mechanik's Savvy so long as you are outdoors and not on or inside a structure or vehicle or underground. A trapper's kit can be used in place of thieves' tools to disable a nonmagical trap.

entries.name: Set a Trap
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you can use your action and expend one use of your Tinkering to set a trap anywhere within 5 feet of you. The DC to spot, detect, or disable one of your traps equals 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier. When you set a trap, you can expend a second use of your Tinkering to roll a Tinkering die and add its total to the DC. If you are setting a premade trap instead, you can set it as a bonus action. Choose one of the following trap types when you set a trap: Disabling Trap (Dex save or Tinkering die damage + exhaustion), Immobilizing Trap (Dex save or {@condition restrained}), or Lethal Trap (Dex save or 3× Tinkering die + proficiency bonus damage, half on success).

subclassFeature.name: Cautious Advance
entries: Beginning at 7th level, you can take a bonus action on each of your turns in combat. This action can be used only to take the Dash, Disengage, or Hide action.

subclassFeature.name: Skilled Trapper
entries: When you reach 10th level, you can use your Set a Trap as a bonus action, rather than as an action. In addition, choose one of the following trap styles: Brutal Trap (add Intelligence modifier to damage), Hidden Trap (double proficiency bonus for detection/disarm DC), or Sturdy Trap (immobilizing trap escape DC +5).

subclassFeature.name: Trapper's Instinct
entries: Beginning at 15th level, you have a sixth sense for avoiding traps. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) and Intelligence (Investigation) checks made to detect traps and secret doors. You also have advantage on saving throws made to avoid or resist traps, as well as resistance to any damage dealt by traps.

subclassFeature.name: Master Trapper
entries: At 18th level, you are a master at setting traps. Choose a second trap style, from those in Skilled Trapper or from: Inescapable Trap (disadvantage on Dex saves to avoid trap effects), Reliable Trap (trap resets if target succeeds on save), or Spiteful Trap (secondary trap triggers when primary is disarmed). You can apply either or both styles to any trap you set. At the end of a long rest, you can replace any trap style you know with another.

entries.name: Rage of the Devourer
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you enter a rage, you can choose to channel the power of the Devourer Wurm. While in this state, your teeth and claws become razor-sharp, allowing you to make unarmed strikes that deal slashing damage equal to 1d6 + your Strength modifier. Whenever you kill a creature that is not a construct or an undead while in this state, you can use your bonus action to devour their still-beating heart, granting you temporary hit points equal to your barbarian level + your Constitution modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Savage Feast
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you use your bonus action to devour a still-beating heart, you can choose to share the feast with an ally within 5 feet of you. Doing so grants that ally temporary hit points equal to your barbarian level.

subclassFeature.name: Chieftain's Presence
entries: Starting at 10th level, you gain a commanding presence that inspires fear in your enemies and gives strength to your allies. While you are raging, your allies within 30 feet of you gain a bonus to their damage rolls equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum of +1).

subclassFeature.name: Beastly Frenzy
entries: Starting at 14th level, when you enter a rage, you can choose to fully embrace the power of the Devourer Wurm. While in this state, your movement speed increases by 10 feet, your unarmed strikes deal 1d8 extra weapon damage, and whenever you score a critical hit with an unarmed strike or a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to make another attack with advantage.

entries.name: Armored Training
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this path, you learn how to channel your natural fury even while covered in heavy plate. You gain proficiency with heavy armor and can gain the benefits of your Rage and Fast Movement features even while wearing heavy armor.

entries.name: Brutalize
entries: Also at 3rd level, you learn how to deal crippling blows that leave your opponents struggling to respond. When you hit a creature with a melee attack while raging, you can choose to impose disadvantage on that creature's next attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Thresher
entries: By 6th level, your training with long, chopping polearms has made you as adept at fighting crowds as you are at taking down large prey. When you use your Reckless Attack, you can use your action to make a melee attack against any number of creatures within your reach, with a separate attack roll for each target. If you do so, you don't have advantage on your attack rolls. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Finisher
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can channel your ferocity to take advantage of blows struck by your allies, allowing you to bring down even the mightiest enemies with a flurry of bloody strikes. While raging, you have advantage on melee attack rolls against any creature that has less than half of its hit point maximum.

subclassFeature.name: Take Down
entries: By 14th level, you've mastered the art of inflicting wounds that wear down your enemies rather than fell them in a single strike. When you hit a creature with a melee attack while raging, you can choose to use this feature as a reaction. If you do so, that creature can't regain hit points for a number of minutes equal to your proficiency bonus. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you join the College of Fauna at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Animal Handling skill if you aren't already proficient in it.

entries.name: Idyllic Recital
entries: At 3rd level, you have the power to tame beasts in addition to humanoids and other sentient beings. As an action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to entrance beasts. Each beast within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you must make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. If you or creatures friendly to you are fighting a beast, it has advantage on the saving throw. On a failed save, the beast is {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute or until it takes damage.

entries.name: Voice of Nature
entries: Also at 3rd level, beasts can understand your speech. Even though most beasts lack the intelligence to understand more than basic concepts, you can use straightforward gestures to convey simple requests or present questions to friendly beasts.

subclassFeature.name: Pack Bond
entries: By 6th level, your connection to animals is strong enough that you can forge bonds beyond simple companionship. Bonding with a friendly beast requires a successful DC 14 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. You can bond with a number of beasts up to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one beast), but their combined challenge rating can't exceed your proficiency bonus. A bonded beast fights alongside you until you or your companions do anything harmful to it, and it acts on its own initiative in combat. A bonded beast gains the Bondmate special trait: it can reroll an attack roll of 1 or 2 against a target creature if the bonded character is within 5 feet of the target.

subclassFeature.name: Primal Refrain
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can reach beyond a being's senses and speak to its primal nature. As an action, you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to entrance one creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that can see or hear you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you for 1 minute or until it takes damage. If the target has an Intelligence of 4 or lower, it has disadvantage on the saving throw. Alternatively, as an action you can expend one use of Bardic Inspiration to instill fear in an audience: each creature within 60 feet must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition frightened} for 1 minute, taking 1d4 psychic damage at the start of each of its turns.

entries.name: Warder's Discipline
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain access to a special pool of dice called defense dice, which are d6s. The number of defense dice in your pool equals your proficiency bonus. A defense die is expended when you use it. You regain all expended defense dice when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Warder's Tactics
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to use your defense dice to hinder your enemies' attacks. When a creature you can see within 30 feet of you makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to reduce the attack roll by the number you roll on your defense die. You can choose to use this feature after the creature makes its roll, but before the GM determines whether the attack hits.

subclassFeature.name: Roadblock
entries: Starting at 7th level, when a creature within 5 feet of you hits a friendly creature with an attack, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to reduce the damage by the number you roll on your defense die + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). You must be wielding a shield or a simple or martial melee weapon to use this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Warder's Stance
entries: At 10th level, you can use a bonus action to enter a defensive stance. When you do so, your speed is halved and you gain the following benefits until you're incapacitated or end the stance as a bonus action: Enhanced Tactics (Warder's Tactics reduces the attack roll by defense die + proficiency bonus), Parry (Roadblock can expend up to proficiency bonus defense dice), and Superior Defense (Dodge action lets you expend one defense die to also take Dash or Disengage as a bonus action). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Guardian's Strike
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can unleash a powerful strike against a creature that tries to harm your allies.
