When a creature within 5 feet of you attacks a friendly creature or targets it with a spell, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to make a weapon attack against the attacking creature. If the attack hits, add the defense die to the attack's damage roll. You must be wielding a shield or a simple or martial melee weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Warder's Aura
entries: At 18th level, as an action you can create a 30-foot-radius sphere that lasts for 1 minute, centered on you and moving with you. For the duration, when an affected friendly creature takes damage, makes a saving throw, or makes an attack roll, you can use your reaction and expend one defense die to reduce the damage by or add to the roll the number you roll + your Constitution modifier (minimum of 1). You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Wilderness Warrior
entries: Beginning when you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Perception and Survival skills, and moving through nonmagical difficult terrain doesn't cost you extra movement.

entries.name: Pack of Wolves
entries: Also at 3rd level, you gain the ability to coordinate attacks with your allies. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a melee attack, you can use a bonus action to allow a friendly creature within 5 feet of you to make a melee attack against the same target without using its reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Power Swell
entries: At 7th level, a wild vitality rushes through you. As a bonus action, you can channel this surge through your allies as well. Until the start of your next turn, you and friendly creatures within 5 feet of you roll one additional weapon damage die when you hit a target with a melee attack. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Hungry like the Wolf
entries: At 10th level, when you score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, you can move up to half your speed immediately after the attack. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Wolf's Senses
entries: At 15th level, your connection to your predatory nature sharpens your senses to a razor's edge. You have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. (If you naturally have darkvision without this feature, the range of your darkvision increases by 60 feet.) Additionally, you have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on smell or hearing.

subclassFeature.name: Beastly Fury
entries: At 18th level, when a creature hits you with a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee weapon attack against that creature. If the attack hits, you can roll one additional weapon damage die.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this tradition, you gain proficiency in the Nature and Survival skills.

entries.name: Crawl
entries: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you become as deadly on the ground as you are on your feet. You can't be knocked prone unless you allow it, and being prone doesn't impose disadvantage on your melee attack rolls.

subclassFeature.name: Impervious Flesh
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you channel your inner predator to make yourself impervious to the attacks of your enemies. When you aren't wearing armor or wielding a shield, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier + your Wisdom modifier. In addition, when you use your Patient Defense, you can spend 1 additional ki point to have resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Grab and Toss
entries: At 6th level, you can fling away your enemies like a wild gator tossing an attacker. When you are grappling a creature, you can use your action to throw it in a straight line up to a number of feet equal to five times your Strength modifier. A creature thrown in this manner takes damage equal to your Martial Arts die and must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone. If a thrown creature strikes a solid surface or another creature, it takes bludgeoning damage equal to your Martial Arts die for every 10 feet it was thrown. A creature struck by the thrown creature must succeed on a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw or take the same damage and be knocked prone.

subclassFeature.name: Patient Predator
entries: By 11th level, you have learned how to wait for the perfect moment to strike, just like the great beasts of the swamp. You have advantage on attack rolls against any creature that has already taken its action this turn.

subclassFeature.name: Gyre
entries: At 17th level, you learn how to mimic the death roll of the great gators of the swamp. You have advantage on checks made to initiate or maintain a grapple. A creature grappled by you is also {@condition restrained}. While a creature is grappled by you, you can spend any number of ki points as a bonus action to enter a death roll. For each ki point you spend, the creature takes an amount of damage equal to your Martial Arts die + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Tenets of the Chosen
entries: By Any Means Necessary: Nothing will stop you from ensuring that no harm comes to those you protect. Duty: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you. Protection: You are the sanctuary of the innocent, and behind your shield, they can find respite.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at certain paladin levels: 3rd (guiding bolt, thunderous smite), 5th (grave wind, hand of fate), 9th (beacon of hope, mirage), 13th (lamentation, stranglehold), 17th (axiomatic spear, wrath of Dhunia).

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Divine Shield
entries: As a bonus action, you can use your Channel Divinity to shield your allies. For 1 minute, each friendly creature within 15 feet of you gains a bonus to AC equal to your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Righteous Vengeance
entries: When a friendly creature within 30 feet of you is damaged by a hostile creature's attack, you can use your reaction to respond with holy indignation. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls you make with melee weapons for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Defense
entries: Starting at 7th level, as a bonus action you can choose one friendly creature within 10 feet of you. You and the chosen creature gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage (your choice) from nonmagical attacks for 1 minute or until you're no longer within 10 feet of each other. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Protector
entries: Starting at 15th level, you can deflect blows directed at your allies. When a friendly creature within 5 feet of you is hit by a melee weapon attack, you can use your reaction to make a melee attack roll against the attacker. If your total equals or exceeds the attacker's total, the attacker's attack is deflected and automatically misses. If you are wielding a shield, you have advantage on the attack roll. If you are wielding a heavy reach weapon and successfully deflect, you can immediately make an opportunity attack against the attacker as part of the same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Defense (30 ft.)
entries: At 18th level, the range of your Aura of Defense increases to 30 feet. Additionally, you no longer need to choose a damage type — you and the chosen creature gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Final Vengeance
entries: At 20th level, your divine purpose fuels your blows. When a friendly creature that you can see is hit by a hostile creature, you can use your reaction to move up to your speed toward the hostile creature and make one attack against it. Any hit you score against the creature counts as a critical hit. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Tenets of the Paragon
entries: Be the Light: Be a glorious beacon for all who live in despair. Let the light of your joy and courage shine forth in all your deeds. Duty: Be responsible for your actions and their consequences, protect those entrusted to your care, and obey those who have just authority over you. Honor: Treat others with fairness, and let your honorable deeds be an example to them.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at certain paladin levels: 3rd (elusive, shield), 5th (flashing blade, sacrosanct, spellpiercer), 9th (beacon of hope, blade of radiance), 13th (freedom of movement, stoneskin), 17th (axiomatic spear, purification).

entries.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you take this oath at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Inspiring Presence
entries: As a bonus action, you can inspire others through your very presence. Choose a number of friendly creatures equal to your proficiency bonus. Each creature gains one Inspiration die, a d6 (becomes d8 at 7th level). Once within the next 10 minutes, a creature can roll the die and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Sacred Weapon
entries: As an action, you can imbue one weapon you are holding with positive energy. For 1 minute, you add your Charisma modifier to attack rolls made with that weapon (minimum bonus of +1). The weapon also emits bright light in a 20-foot radius and dim light 20 feet beyond that.

subclassFeature.name: Paragon's Example
entries: Starting at 7th level, choose one of the following ideals. When you use this feature, choose a number of friendly creatures up to your Charisma modifier (minimum one creature) within 10 feet of you. For the next 10 minutes, you and the chosen creatures gain the benefits described below.

entries.name: Bravery
entries: Affected creatures can't be {@condition charmed}. If already charmed, the condition ends.

entries.name: Strength
entries: Affected creatures double their Strength modifier on attack damage rolls and have advantage on Strength and Constitution saving throws.

entries.name: Wisdom
entries: Affected creatures double their Wisdom modifier (unless negative) on Wisdom checks and have advantage on Wisdom saving throws.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Retaliatory Thrust
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you take damage from a creature that is within 10 feet of you, you can use your reaction to move up to 10 feet toward that creature and then make one melee weapon attack against it. If the attack hits, the creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be knocked prone. You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Paragon's Example (30 ft.)
entries: At 18th level, the range of your Paragon's Example increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Mastered Strike
entries: At 20th level, divine fury guides your strikes. You can choose to use this feature at the beginning of a combat encounter, when everyone rolls initiative. If you do, during the first round of combat your first weapon attack scores a critical hit on a roll of 18–20, and if you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can use a bonus action to force the target to lose all its damage resistances until the end of your next turn. You can use this feature three times. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Mount Mastery
entries: At 3rd level, you gain proficiency with Animal Handling and gain a Skirovik mountain goat (a giant goat) as your mount. While riding your mount, you have advantage on initiative rolls, and your mount gains: speed +10 ft., can move through difficult terrain without penalty, and can make a Ram attack as a bonus action dealing 1d8 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: Elemental Shroud
entries: At 3rd level, as a bonus action, you can create a shroud of fog or sheets of rain that grants you advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks to hide. While shrouded, creatures have disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks to see you. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once). You regain all uses after finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Lightning Strike
entries: At 7th level, you gain the ability to strike your opponents with flashes of lightning. When you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can choose to deal an additional 1d6 lightning damage to the target. Additionally, if the target is wearing metal armor or carrying a metal shield, it takes an additional 1d6 lightning damage.

subclassFeature.name: Fog Step
entries: At 11th level, as a bonus action, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that is obscured by fog, mist, rain, or natural darkness. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum once). You regain all uses after finishing a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Storm's Fury
entries: At 15th level, as an action, you can create a 20-foot-radius sphere of swirling winds and driving rain centered on yourself. Each creature within the sphere must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC or take 4d8 bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. On a successful save, the creature takes half damage and isn't knocked prone. You can use this ability once per long rest.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in the Stealth skill. If you are already proficient in this skill, double your proficiency bonus when you make an ability check with the skill.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Work
entries: At 7th level, you have perfected the ability to deftly switch from melee attacks to ranged attacks. When you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack, you can use your reaction to make a single ranged attack against another creature.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Critical
entries: At 11th level, your weapon attacks score a critical hit on a roll of 19 or 20.

subclassFeature.name: Snap Fire
entries: At 11th level, your mastery of the double crossbow allows you to fire an unnatural number of bolts in a spectacular display of trigger discipline. In the first round of a combat, you can treat your double crossbow as if it were affected by the endless magazine spell and can ignore the loading property of a dual crossbow. You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sprint
entries: At 15th level, if you reduce a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you can use a bonus action to move up to your speed without provoking opportunity attacks. You can use this feature three times. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Soothing Song
entries: At 3rd level, your native ability to nurture warbeasts allows you to put them at ease and make them more compliant. When a friendly warlock makes a Wisdom (Animal Handling) check to keep a warbeast under control and you are within the warlock's control range, you can use your reaction to aid the warlock's efforts, giving the warlock advantage on the ability check.

subclassFeature.name: Resonance Extension
entries: At 7th level, if you are within a friendly warlock's control range, you can use a bonus action to increase the warlock's control range by 10 feet × your proficiency bonus. This effect lasts for a number of rounds equal to your proficiency bonus. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Warbeast Conditioning
entries: At 11th level, as a bonus action, you can touch a friendly warbeast and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. If the check succeeds, you can use one of the following options: Animus Adept (cast the warbeast's animus once without using an action or bonus action), Calming Touch (remove 1 fury point from the warbeast), or Healing Caress (cast cure wounds as a 1st-level spell on the warbeast without using a spell slot). You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Furious Attacker
entries: At 15th level, as a bonus action, you can touch a friendly warbeast and make a DC 15 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check. If the check succeeds, you can remove 1 fury point from the warbeast to use one of the following abilities: Attack Boost (advantage on your next attack roll), Damage Boost (next hit deals extra 1d8 damage), or Shake It Off (immediately remake a saving throw against an ongoing effect, with advantage). You can use this feature twice. You regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiency
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Animal Handling, Intimidation, or Survival.

entries.name: Mounted Marauder
entries: At 3rd level, your command of your mount makes it your most reliable accomplice. When you use your Cunning Action, you can mount a creature that is within 5 feet of you or dismount as part of your bonus action, and doing so doesn't cost you any movement or provoke opportunity attacks.

entries.name: Ambush Attack
entries: Starting at 3rd level, if you take the Attack action while mounted and your steed moves at least 20 feet in a straight line before the attack, you can use your Sneak Attack against the target even if you don't have advantage on the attack roll, but not if you have disadvantage on it.

subclassFeature.name: Unwelcome Guest
entries: At 9th level, you master the art of getting away. When you or your mount are attacked while mounted, you can use your reaction immediately after the attack to force your mount to move up to half its speed, without provoking opportunity attacks. In addition, if you are unmounted and your steed moves within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to mount your steed as it passes by.

subclassFeature.name: Sidesaddle Slasher
entries: At 13th level, your skill as a rider allows you to use your mount's mass to extend your attack range. On your turn, when you make an attack with a one-handed melee weapon while mounted, you add 5 feet to your reach.

subclassFeature.name: Blood on the Saddle
entries: Beginning at 17th level, your mount's bond with you extends all the way to death's door. When you are reduced to 0 hit points while mounted but not killed outright, you aren't dismounted unless the triggering effect says otherwise. If you fail a death saving throw while mounted, you can reroll it with advantage (once per long rest). After you make a death saving throw while mounted, you can issue your mount a brief command.

entries.name: Poacher's Instincts
entries: Starting at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you gain proficiency in the Deception and Survival skills.

entries.name: Trap Ambush
entries: Also at 3rd level, your cunningly placed traps can deal devastating damage. When a creature triggers one of your traps, you can add your Sneak Attack damage to the damage dealt by the trap.

entries.name: Trapmaker
entries: At 3rd level, you gain the ability to craft simple traps and snares out of natural materials found in the wilderness. You can spend 10 minutes constructing a trap that is equivalent to a hunting trap. The trap remains active for 24 hours or until triggered.

subclassFeature.name: Camouflage
entries: At 9th level, you gain the ability to blend in with your surroundings. When you are in an environment that contains natural elements such as rocks, trees, or grass, you can use your action to camouflage yourself. While camouflaged, you have advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to remain hidden. The camouflage lasts for 1 hour or until you take the Dash action or are hit by an attack or spell.

subclassFeature.name: Acute Senses
entries: At 13th level, your senses are sharp enough that you can perceive the world around you with great clarity. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks made to detect hidden creatures or objects, and you can see in dim light as if it were bright light and in darkness as if it were dim light.

subclassFeature.name: Master Hunter
entries: At 17th level, you are a master of the hunt. The time you must spend picking up a creature's trail is halved. You can use your action to mark a creature you are tracking, giving you advantage on Wisdom (Survival) checks to track it and attack rolls against it for 1 hour or until it is captured or killed.

entries.name: Arcanivore Magic
entries: Starting when you choose this origin at 1st level, you learn additional spells as you level: 1st (faerie fire, find familiar), 3rd (arcanist's magic aura, locate object), 5th (glyph of warding, nondetection), 7th (arcane eye, locate creature), 9th (arcane hand, legend lore). Each counts as a sorcerer spell but doesn't count against your spells known.

entries.name: Hedge Ward
entries: Also starting at 1st level, you have advantage on saving throws against cantrips. Additionally, at 2nd level, when another creature within 5 feet of you casts a spell of 1st level or higher, or when another creature targets you with a spell other than a cantrip, you can use your reaction to regain a number of sorcery points equal to the level of the spell cast.

subclassFeature.name: Energy Store
entries: At 6th level, your innate ability to manipulate magical forces is strong enough that you can touch the essence of those close to you. As part of a long rest, you can channel unexpended sorcery points into a willing living creature (typically a familiar). You can't channel more than half your maximum sorcery points (rounded up) in this way. As a bonus action, you can touch the creature and draw sorcery points from the pool stored inside it.

subclassFeature.name: Devour Spell
entries: At 14th level, when a spell of 5th level or lower is cast within 5 feet of you, or if you are the only target of a spell of 5th level or lower, you can use your reaction to absorb the spell harmlessly into yourself, completely negating it. In addition, until you finish a long rest, you can use sorcery points (as shown in the Creating Spell Slots table) to cast that spell but can't cast the spell by expending spell slots. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Disenchanting Grasp
entries: At 18th level, as an action, you can regain hit points by draining the enchantment from an item you're holding or that is held by a creature you have successfully grappled. The magic item makes a saving throw against your spell save DC (with a bonus based on rarity: Common +1, Uncommon +2, Rare +3, Very Rare +4, Legendary +5, Unique +6). On a failed save, the item loses all magical properties for 1d4 rounds and you regain hit points (1d6 per rarity tier). An item drained for three consecutive turns permanently loses all its magical properties. You can use this ability a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier. You regain all expended uses after you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Twin Magic
entries: At 1st level, you learn additional spells as you level: 1st (cold-blooded, swamp pit), 3rd (acid arrow, bilious fog), 5th (bestow curse, conjure animals), 7th (control water, giant insect), 9th (black tide, contagion). Each counts as a sorcerer spell but doesn't count against your spells known.

entries.name: Lil' Buddy
entries: Also at 1st level, you have a conjoined twin with whom you share a spiritual link. You have advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks and on saving throws against being {@condition blinded}, {@condition charmed}, {@condition deafened}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition stunned}, and knocked {@condition unconscious}.

subclassFeature.name: Share the Load
entries: At 6th level, your twin helps you cast spells. When you cast a cantrip and use your Twinned Spell to target a second creature, doing so doesn't cost you any sorcery points.

subclassFeature.name: Two Heads Are Better than One
entries: At 14th level, your twin can take over the focus of spells you cast together. You can now concentrate on two spells at once.

subclassFeature.name: Double Down
entries: Upon reaching 18th level, your twinned soul grants you enhanced mastery over your sorcerous magic. When you spend sorcery points, roll a d20. On a roll of 11 or higher, you immediately gain 1 sorcery point.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Maw lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell: 1st (purify food and drink, inflict wounds), 2nd (darkvision, locate animals or plants), 3rd (nondetection, rift), 4th (compulsion, locate creature), 5th (maelstrom, wall of force).

entries.name: Multivore
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can gain sustenance from anything you ingest. As an action, you can eat any solid matter to gain temporary hit points equal to 1d4 + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). This strengthens your teeth and jaw, allowing you to chew and ingest wood and leather without difficulty. You can gain temporary hit points from this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Predatory Awareness
entries: At 6th level, the growing hunger inside of you heightens your senses. You gain proficiency in your choice of two of the following skills: Investigation, Perception, or Survival. Alternatively, you gain proficiency in one of those skills and double your proficiency bonus on checks made with that skill. In addition, your constant awareness means you can't be surprised, and you gain blindsight out to a range of 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Cannibal Spirit
entries: At 10th level, your connection to the Maw is so strong that the supernatural hunger inside you begins feeding on your body, leaving lean muscle behind. Your Strength or Dexterity score and ability score maximum increase by 1, and your walking speed increases by 10 feet. Additionally, you have advantage on Charisma (Intimidation) rolls against Medium or smaller creatures.

subclassFeature.name: The Hungering Void
entries: Beginning at 14th level, as an action, you can become a gateway into the hungry abyss until the start of your next turn. You can't take any additional actions, bonus actions, or reactions on the turn you use this feature, and you can't maintain concentration spells. Beginning with the creature closest to you, each creature within 20 feet of you must make a Strength saving throw against your spell save DC. A creature within 10 feet of you has disadvantage on the saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is pulled 10 feet toward you (20 feet if it fails by 10 or more). On a successful save, it isn't pulled.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at certain shaman levels: 1st (command, false life), 3rd (darkness, stir the dead), 5th (death pact, ichor of Kossk), 7th (devour spirit, phantasmal killer), 9th (antilife shell, raise dead).

entries.name: Cold-Blooded
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you become somewhat disconnected from the world of the living. You have advantage on Charisma saving throws against being {@condition charmed}.

entries.name: Grave Secrets
entries: Also at 1st level, you gain proficiency in your choice of the Arcana skill or the Intimidation skill, and you have advantage on ability checks made with the chosen skill.

entries.name: Resist the Call
entries: At 1st level, as an action, you can chant an exhortation that helps your companions stave off death. Each friendly creature within 30 feet of you that can hear you has advantage on death saving throws until the end of your next turn. In addition, if the creature is frightened, paralyzed, petrified, or stunned, the condition ends. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Shamanic Rites: Grave Charms
entries: At 2nd level, you learn a new rite. Choose either Terminal Whispers or Unhallow.

entries.name: Terminal Whispers
entries: When a living creature dies within 5 feet of you, you can use your reaction to draw upon its spirit energy. If you do so, you can roll a d4 and add the number rolled to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn.

entries.name: Unhallow
entries: As an action, you call upon the spirits in your area to curse and vex your foes. For 1 minute, each hostile creature within 30 feet of you has disadvantage on concentration rolls to maintain spells, and each friendly creature within 30 feet of you has advantage on saving throws to avoid being affected by spells cast by hostile creatures.

subclassFeature.name: Bone Shaker
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you can channel the power of the grave to take control of the flesh and bones of living creatures. When you deal damage to a creature other than a construct, you can force the creature to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, you can use your reaction to take temporary control of the creature and can immediately force it to move up to its speed and make a single melee attack, after which your control ends. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest. Beginning at 14th level, you can use this feature twice between rests.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Goods
entries: Beginning at 6th level, your melee attacks count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

subclassFeature.name: Rise
entries: At 8th level, you can use an action to reanimate a humanoid within 5 feet of you that has died within the last hour. To determine the creature's hit points, roll a number of Hit Dice equal to your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier (minimum one Hit Die). Once you do so, you must finish a long rest before you can use this feature again (except as noted in Refuse the Call). The reanimated creature becomes undead with Turn Resistance and Unliving Flesh, but automatically fails death saving throws and can no longer be raised if it dies in this state. At the end of each long rest, you must sacrifice spell slots (combined level ≥ 1d4 per creature) to maintain each reanimated creature.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Aspect
entries: At 17th level, you are suffused with the energies of the grave. You are immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition and have immunity to necrotic damage and poison damage.

subclassFeature.name: Refuse the Call
entries: Upon reaching 17th level, whenever you start your turn with 0 hit points and would normally make your first death saving throw, you can use your Rise on yourself if you have not already used it since your last long rest. The effects take place immediately. While you are reanimated in this way, you can't use your Rise on yourself or another creature but can still sacrifice spell slots to maintain reanimated creatures.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: You gain domain spells at certain shaman levels: 1st (create or destroy water, pestilent wind), 3rd (darkness, pass without trace), 5th (bestow curse, glimpse of Sike Dulra), 7th (control water, divination), 9th (Ashiga's breath, mislead).

entries.name: Bonus Cantrip
entries: When you choose this domain at 1st level, you learn one cantrip of your choice from the following list: acid splash, friends, guidance, or minor illusion. This cantrip counts as a shaman cantrip for you but doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.

entries.name: Influence
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose one creature within 5 feet of you. Until the end of your next turn, that creature has advantage on its next ability check, attack roll, or saving throw. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Shamanic Rites: Dreams of Ashiga
entries: At 2nd level, you learn a new rite. Choose either Call the Mists or Hazy Portents.

entries.name: Call the Mists
entries: As an action, you create a 60-foot-radius sphere of fog that lasts for a number of rounds equal to half your shaman level (rounded up), moving with you. Choose a number of creatures up to your Wisdom modifier. You and the chosen creatures gain a bonus to AC equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum +1) against attacks made by creatures more than 5 feet away. You and the chosen creatures can see through the fog clearly, but its area is heavily obscured for all other creatures.

entries.name: Hazy Portents
entries: As a reaction, you can force one creature within 30 feet of you to reroll an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, but the creature must use the new roll.

subclassFeature.name: Protection of the Mists
entries: By 6th level, you have mastered the art of using your trade to protect yourself and your allies. When you gain a bonus to AC from your Ritual Protections, you can choose a number of friendly creatures up to your Wisdom modifier (minimum one creature). Until the start of your next turn, each chosen creature gains the same bonus to AC if it is within 30 feet of you.

subclassFeature.name: Mirage
entries: Beginning at 8th level, you become an expert at being in one place while appearing to be in another. When you are hit by an attack, you can choose to ignore the effects of that attack. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Dark Waters
entries: Beginning at 17th level, as a bonus action, you can expend a spell slot to call up dark water from the earth, turning the land around you into marsh in a 30-foot aura. The aura lasts for a number of rounds equal to the level of the spell slot expended and moves with you. For the duration, the area is difficult terrain for hostile creatures, and each friendly creature in the area (including you) has half cover against attacks from within 5 feet and three-quarters cover against attacks from more than 5 feet away.

entries.name: Domain Spells
entries: You gain resonance spells at certain warlock levels: 1st (mud foot), 3rd (dirge of mists), 5th (acid arrow), 10th (death pact), 13th (devour spirit), 17th (absorb flesh).

entries.name: Bestial Connection
entries: At 1st level, animal friendship and speak with animals are added to your known spells but can't be replaced when you gain a level. You can cast animal friendship, speak with animals, and animal messenger without spending fury points, but you can only target blackhides, boneswarms, cane leeches, ironbacks, snappers, swamp horrors, and tatzylwurms with these spells. At 3rd level, animal messenger is added to your known spells. Additionally, you can bond to one swamp warbeast without counting the bond against the number of items you can attune to.

entries.name: Patient Hunter
entries: Starting at 1st level, when a warbeast that is under your control and in your control range makes an opportunity attack, you can use your reaction to give that warbeast advantage on the attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus. You regain all expended uses after you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Flesh of the Fallen
entries: At 6th level, when you or a warbeast that is under your control and in your control range reduces a hostile creature other than a construct or an undead to 0 hit points, you can use a bonus action to consume some of the creature's flesh or have the warbeast do so. Consuming the flesh regains 1d8 hit points for each point of the creature's Constitution modifier (minimum 1d8 hit points).

subclassFeature.name: Death Roll
entries: Starting at 10th level, when a warbeast that is under your control and in your control range reduces a hostile creature to 0 hit points, you can use your reaction to force the warbeast to move up to 15 feet and then make one melee attack against a hostile creature within its reach. This movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Cold-Blooded Cunning
entries: Starting at 14th level, as a bonus action, you can spend 1 fury point to give each warbeast that is under your control and in your control range advantage on saving throws to avoid being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Reptilian Hide
entries: At 18th level, as a bonus action, you can spend 2 fury points to give each warbeast that is under your control and in your control range resistance to nonmagical bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Blade of Torment
entries: Requires attunement. This enchantment can be applied to any sword. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you hit a creature with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 1d8} psychic damage, and if it is a warbeast, you can choose to add 1 fury point to it or remove 1 fury point from it. In addition, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Dexterity modifier. On a failed save, the creature is {@condition paralyzed} for 1 minute. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

item.name: Bokor's Staff
entries: Adorned with grisly trophies, this staff grants its wielder the power to silence an enemy's spells. When you hit a creature with this magic weapon, the target must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or be unable to cast spells or use an animus until the start of your next turn.

item.name: Cloak of the Fog Drake
entries: Wondrous Item. This cloak is made from the scales of a fog drake. While wearing it, you can breathe underwater. In addition, while wearing the cloak, you can use an action to cast the {@spell fog cloud} spell from it as a 3rd-level spell. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Croak-Throat Bagpipes
entries: Wondrous Item. These eerie bagpipes are made from the dried skin and esophagus of a croak. While playing the bagpipes, you can use an action to cast the {@spell fear} spell from them as a 3rd-level spell. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Crystal Skull
entries: Wondrous Item. This mysterious crystal skull is said to hold the knowledge of ancient civilizations long since lost to time. The skull is made of a clear, flawless crystal that glows with an otherworldly light.

When holding the skull, you can understand and speak any language and can read and write any script. You also gain proficiency in any one skill or tool of your choice, and you double your proficiency bonus on any ability check made using that skill or tool.

Additionally, you can use an action to cast the {@spell legend lore} spell, allowing you to gain knowledge about a specific person, place, or object. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Dhunia's Rage
entries: Requires attunement by a follower of Dhunia. This enchantment can be applied to any axe. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.
When you hit with it, you can choose to maximize your weapon damage dice against the target. The weapon can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

item.name: Dhunia's Touch
entries: Requires attunement by a follower of Dhunia. This enchantment can be applied to any armor. While wearing this armor, you can speak its command word as a reaction when you are hit by an attack or fail a saving throw, but before damage is rolled. If you do so, you take no damage from the attack or effect. The armor can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

item.name: Exhumator
entries: Requires attunement. This enchantment can be applied to any weapon. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon.

When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with this magic weapon, you can choose to raise the target as an undead under your control for 1 round. The creature's type becomes undead, it regains 1 hit point, it obeys your commands to the best of its ability, and it remains under your control until the end of your next turn or it is destroyed.

Additionally, you can choose a creature you are controlling with this weapon and channel a spell through it as if it were a channeler. The creature must be within 30 feet of you, and any spell cast through it will originate from its location. If you channel a spell through the creature, it is destroyed after the spell is cast.

item.name: Gravelight Candle
entries: Wondrous Item. This candle is made of a mixture of beeswax and the tallow of a humanoid creature, all of which is infused with tiny fragments of arcane minerals. The candle's magic is activated when the candle is lit, which requires an action. After burning for 4 hours, the candle is destroyed. You can snuff it out early for use at a later time. Deduct the time it burned in increments of 1 minute from the candle's total burn time.

While lit, the candle sheds bright light in a 5-foot radius and dim light for an additional 5 feet. Any hidden door, trap, or secret compartment within that light is revealed, and any invisible creature or object within that light becomes visible. Additionally, while the candle is lit and you are holding it, you have darkvision out to a range of 60 feet.

item.name: Leech Bite
entries: This enchantment can be applied to any ranged weapon. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage, and you regain a number of hit points equal to half the necrotic damage dealt.

item.name: Life Drinker
entries: Requires attunement. This enchantment can be applied to any weapon. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with it, you gain a number of temporary hit points equal to half the damage dealt.

item.name: Life Trader
entries: Requires attunement. This enchantment can be applied to any weapon. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with it, you can choose to sacrifice your own hit points to deal extra damage. For every 2 hit points you sacrifice, the weapon deals an extra {@damage 1d4} necrotic damage to the target. The number of hit points you can sacrifice is limited by your current hit point maximum, and you can't sacrifice hit points if doing so would cause you to drop to 0 hit points.

item.name: Mind-Clouding Maul
entries: You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 1d6} psychic damage and must make a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. On a failed save, the creature's spellcasting ability is impaired. For the next minute, it has disadvantage on any spell attack rolls it makes to cast a spell, and targets have advantage on saving throws against any spell the creature casts, unless the creature spends an additional spell slot of 1st level or higher to cast it.

item.name: Skull Prayer Beads
entries: Wondrous Item. This macabre necklace is made of humanoid skulls strung together on a cord. Each skull is intricately carved with dark arcane symbols and runes. The necklace is imbued with necromantic energy.

While wearing this necklace, you can communicate with the spirits of the dead as if you had cast the {@spell speak with dead} spell, and you can use an action to cast the {@spell animate dead} spell from the necklace as a 3rd-level spell. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Soul Consumer
entries: Requires attunement. This enchantment can be applied to any weapon. You gain a +3 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 2d8} force damage, and you can choose to absorb a part of the creature's soul, gaining a number of temporary hit points equal to the force damage dealt.

You can use an action to release some of the soul energy stored in the weapon, which escapes with a sound like a tormented scream. If you do, you have advantage on your next attack roll, ability check, or saving throw. Once used, this property can't be used again until the next dawn.

item.name: Soul-Fueled Blade
entries: Requires attunement. This enchantment can be applied to any sword. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you reduce a creature other than a construct or an undead with a soul to 0 hit points with this magic weapon, you gain one spell slot of the highest level you can cast. This spell slot expires if it is not used within 10 minutes. While the extra spell slot is active, the sword is outlined in a faint orange glow.

item.name: Staff of the Winter Crone
entries: Requires attunement. This twisted, gnarled staff is a relic of the Northkin tribes of trollkin. It is made of dark wood that always drips with jagged ice, regardless of the weather.
This staff can be wielded as a magic quarterstaff that grants a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it.
The staff has 6 charges for the following properties, which you can use while you are holding the staff. The staff regains {@dice 1d6} expended charges daily at dawn.

entries.name: Icy Grip
entries: You can use an action to expend 1 charge to force a creature you can see within 60 feet of you to make a DC 15 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the target is {@condition restrained} for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.

entries.name: Snowfall
entries: You can use an action to expend 1 charge to cause snow to fall in a 20-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder centered on a point you can see within 60 feet of you. The snow lasts for 1 hour or until you use an action to dismiss it.

entries.name: Spells
entries: You can use an action to expend 1 charge to cast one of the following spells without expending a spell slot: {@spell control weather} (limited to winter weather), {@spell fog cloud}, or protection from cold.

item.name: Thunderstrike Maul
entries: This massive maul is crafted from a rare metal that has been struck by lightning. It is imbued with the power of thunder and crackles with electrical energy. When you hit a creature with an attack using this magic weapon, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} thunder damage and must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. On a failed save, the target becomes {@condition deafened} for 1 minute.

While wielding this weapon, you can cast the {@spell thunderwave} spell from it as a 2nd-level spell. The DC is equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier. The weapon can't be used this way again until the next dawn.

item.name: Wintertide
entries: Nyss claymore. You gain a +2 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a creature with it, that creature takes an extra {@damage 2d6} cold damage.

If you score a critical hit against a creature with this magic weapon, it must make a Constitution saving throw with a DC equal to 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength or Dexterity modifier. On a failed save, the target is frozen solid, rendering it {@condition incapacitated} until the end of your next turn.

item.name: Bog Trog Armor
entries: Light armor made from the natural materials of swamp environments. AC 11 + Dexterity modifier.

item.name: Nyss Leathers
entries: Supple armor crafted by the Nyss from cold-treated hides. AC 13 + Dexterity modifier.

item.name: Hunter's Armor
entries: Medium armor crafted for wilderness hunters. AC 15 + Dexterity modifier (max 2). Requires Strength 15.

item.name: Spiked Buckler
entries: Shield (+1 AC). The spiked face of this buckler can also be used to make melee attacks. When you hit with it, you deal {@damage 1d4} piercing damage.

item.name: Cleft Dagger
entries: This forked dagger has two tines. When you score a critical hit with this weapon, you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} piercing damage (Cleft property).

item.name: Fish Hook
entries: A long-handled hook used for fishing and combat alike.

item.name: Ice Axe
entries: A hand tool designed for climbing ice and snow, repurposed as a weapon. When used to climb, you have advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks.

item.name: Punch Dagger
entries: A short blade mounted on a horizontal handle, designed to extend from a closed fist. You are considered armed while wearing this weapon. When you make an unarmed strike, you can deal {@damage 1d6} piercing damage instead of the normal bludgeoning damage.

item.name: Swamp Hook
entries: A curved blade on a short handle used for cutting through dense vegetation and for combat. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can use your bonus action to attempt to disarm it (opposed Strength check).

item.name: Tomahawk
entries: A lightweight throwing axe.

item.name: Blowgun
entries: A hollow tube used to shoot small darts by blowing through one end.

item.name: Snare Gun
entries: A small firearm that fires a weighted snare net. Instead of dealing damage, a successful hit forces the target to make a DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition restrained} until it frees itself. A creature can use its action to make a DC 13 Strength check, freeing itself or another creature within its reach on a success. Dealing 5 slashing damage to the snare (AC 10) also frees the creature.

item.name: Scattergun, Heavy
entries: A large-bore firearm that fires a spread of shot. If you do not have a Strength score of 16 or higher, you have disadvantage on attack rolls made with this weapon.

item.name: Speargun
entries: A pneumatic device that launches a spear-like bolt. This weapon is waterproof and functions normally underwater.

item.name: Barbed Hook
entries: A large hook with barbed edges. When you hit a creature with this weapon, the barbs make it difficult to remove. Until the barbs are removed (requiring a DC 13 Strength check as an action), the creature has disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws.

item.name: Cleft Spear
entries: A spear with a forked tip. When you score a critical hit with this weapon, you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} piercing damage (Cleft property).

item.name: Cleft Sword
entries: A sword with a forked blade. When you score a critical hit with this weapon, you deal an extra {@damage 1d4} slashing damage (Cleft property).

item.name: Hook Flail
entries: A flail with a hooked chain. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can use your bonus action to attempt to pull it 5 feet toward you (DC 13 Strength saving throw to resist).

item.name: Kelkax
entries: A long polearm with a spiraling blade favored by gatorfolk. When you hit a creature with this weapon on your turn, you can use your bonus action to make an additional attack with the blunt end, dealing {@damage 1d4} bludgeoning damage on a hit.

item.name: Lyosys
entries: A slender Nyss blade balanced for quick strikes.

item.name: Pole Cleaver
entries: A massive polearm with a broad cleaving blade.

item.name: Sawtooth Axe
entries: A massive axe with a serrated edge. When you hit a creature with this weapon, you can use your bonus action to make an additional raking attack with the serrated edge, dealing {@damage 1d4} slashing damage on a hit.

item.name: Spine Ripper Gauntlet
entries: A gauntlet studded with razor-sharp spines. You are considered armed while wearing this weapon. You can make unarmed strikes with this gauntlet, dealing {@damage 1d8} slashing damage instead of the normal bludgeoning damage for an unarmed strike.

item.name: War Fan
entries: An ornate iron fan that doubles as a weapon and shield. While closed, the fan deals {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage. As a bonus action, you can open the fan; while open it deals {@damage 1d6} slashing damage, and you gain a +2 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn or until you make another attack with the fan. You can close the fan again as a bonus action.

item.name: Dual Crossbow
entries: A double-barreled crossbow that can fire two bolts in quick succession. When you use this weapon to make a ranged attack against a target, you can use your bonus action to make a second ranged attack against the same target.

item.name: Kossite Shortbow
entries: A recurve bow favored by Kossite hunters. Once per turn, you can use your bonus action to make an additional ranged attack with this bow, but that attack is made with disadvantage.

item.name: Air Bladder
entries: A watertight bladder that can be filled with air. When submerged in water, you can breathe air from this bladder. The bladder holds enough air for one Small or Medium creature to breathe for 10 minutes.

item.name: Atlatl
entries: A throwing board used to launch javelins with greater force. When you use an atlatl to throw a javelin, its normal range increases to 60 feet and its long range increases to 180 feet.

item.name: Barbed Arrows (10)
entries: These arrows have hooked heads designed to tear flesh. When you score a critical hit with a barbed arrow, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d4} slashing damage as the arrow's barbs catch and rip.

item.name: Concussion Arrows (10)
entries: Arrows tipped with alchemical bladders filled with concussive compounds. When you score a critical hit with a concussion arrow against a Medium or smaller creature, the creature must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or be {@condition stunned} until the end of its next turn.

item.name: Grappling Arrow
entries: An arrow attached to 50 feet of cord rated to hold 150 pounds. When this arrow hits a surface, it embeds itself securely enough to support climbing.

item.name: Signal Arrows (10)
entries: Arrows that whistle loudly when fired. The sound can be heard up to 1 mile away.

item.name: Animal Call
entries: A small device that mimics the calls of animals. When used, it produces a convincing imitation of a specific creature's call. You can use an action to make a DC 10 Wisdom (Animal Handling) check to attract a creature of the appropriate type, or a DC 14 Dexterity (Stealth) check to use the call to distract one.

item.name: Camouflage Netting
entries: A net woven with natural materials to blend into wilderness environments. While you are beneath or wrapped in this netting in an appropriate environment, you are lightly obscured.

item.name: Flintstriker
entries: A reliable sparking device for starting fires. You can use an action to strike sparks with this device and automatically succeed on checks to light a fire, provided you have appropriate tinder.

item.name: Inflatable Raft Pack
entries: A compressed pack that inflates into a small raft in 2 minutes. The raft can carry up to 4 Medium passengers and their gear.

item.name: Sapper Mine
entries: A pressure-triggered explosive buried in the ground. When a creature steps on the pressure plate, the mine detonates. Each creature within 10 feet must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a success.

item.name: Snow Goggles
entries: Narrow-slit goggles that prevent snow blindness. While wearing these goggles in snowy or bright arctic conditions, you are not affected by snow blindness.

item.name: Snowshoes
entries: Flat frames attached to boots for walking on snow. While wearing snowshoes, deep snow is not difficult terrain for you.

item.name: Wasp Basket
entries: A sealed basket containing an angry insect swarm. You can use an action to throw the basket up to 20 feet. The basket shatters on impact, releasing a swarm of insects. Use the swarm of insects statistics, except the swarm occupies the space where the basket landed and acts on initiative count 0 each round.

item.name: Arrato Seeds
entries: Remedy (Ingested). These small seeds can be chewed before or after a period of sleep deprivation. For 8 hours after consuming these seeds, you have advantage on Constitution saving throws made to avoid exhaustion caused by sleep deprivation.

item.name: Ashiga's Tears
entries: Poison (Ingested). A creature that ingests this poison must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 24 hours. While poisoned in this way, the creature is also {@condition unconscious}. The creature wakes up if it takes damage.

item.name: Blackroot Balm
entries: Remedy (Contact). When this balm is applied to a creature that has been stabilized but not yet restored to consciousness, that creature regains 1 hit point after 10 minutes instead of after 1d4 hours.

item.name: Blindbrew
entries: Poison (Ingested). A creature that ingests this substance must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or become {@condition blinded} for 24 hours. A poisoned creature can repeat the saving throw once per hour, ending the effect on a success.

item.name: Blisterbalm
entries: Poison (Contact). A creature that comes into contact with this substance must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d4} poison damage and have disadvantage on Dexterity saving throws for 24 hours.

item.name: Dvrydal Root
entries: Remedy (Contact). When this root is applied to a creature's skin, that creature has advantage on saving throws against naturally occurring (non-magical) diseases for 24 hours.

item.name: Fly Amanita
entries: Poison (Ingested). A creature that ingests this mushroom must succeed on a DC 15 Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 4 hours and take {@damage 4d6} psychic damage.

item.name: Heron's Maw
entries: Remedy (Ingested). A creature that consumes this herb gains {@dice 2d4} temporary hit points. These temporary hit points last until the creature finishes a short or long rest.

item.name: Horn Moss
entries: Remedy (Ingested). When consumed during a short rest, a creature can reroll one of its Hit Dice, taking the higher result.

item.name: Kaargrot
entries: Poison (Ingested/Inhaled). A creature exposed to this substance must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or be {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour. If the creature fails the save by 5 or more, it is also {@condition unconscious} for the duration.

item.name: Lockjoint
entries: Poison (Injury). A creature subjected to this poison must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or be {@condition paralyzed} for 1 hour.

item.name: Nightshade Oil
entries: Poison (Ingested). A creature that ingests this oil must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 3d8} poison damage and be {@condition poisoned} for 24 hours. While poisoned in this way, the creature experiences vivid hallucinations.

item.name: Ratwort
entries: Remedy (Ingested). A creature that consumes this herb automatically succeeds on its next Constitution saving throw against a disease within 1 hour, provided the herb was consumed at least 1 hour before the save.

item.name: Scarbane
entries: Poison (Contact). A creature that comes into contact with this substance must succeed on a DC 14 Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 1d10} poison damage and be {@condition poisoned} for 1 hour. If the target is a trollkin, it also loses its Trollkin Toughness regeneration for that duration but instead regains 1 hit point at the start of each of its turns for 5 minutes.

item.name: Tamer's Growth
entries: Remedy (Ingested). A creature that consumes this herb becomes more receptive to handlers. For 1 hour after consuming it, any creature that also consumes the herb within that hour has advantage on Wisdom (Animal Handling) checks made against the consuming creature.

item.name: Alchemical Mask
entries: Warbeast Gear. This reinforced mask is fitted to a warbeast's face and filters harmful airborne substances. While wearing this mask, a warbeast is immune to the {@condition poisoned} condition caused by inhaled poisons and has advantage on Constitution saving throws against inhaled substances.

item.name: Alchemical Mortar Shell
entries: Warbeast Gear. Ammunition for the Alchemical Mortar. Three variants exist:

entries.name: Acid Blast
entries: On a hit, the target and each creature within 5 feet of it take {@damage 2d6} acid damage.

entries.name: Psychoactive Gas
entries: On a hit, the shell releases a 15-foot-radius cloud of gas that lasts until the start of the warbeast's next turn. Any creature that enters or starts its turn in the cloud must succeed on a DC 13 Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition frightened} of the warbeast until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Smoke Shell
entries: On a hit, the shell releases a 20-foot-radius sphere of thick smoke that heavily obscures the area until the start of the warbeast's next turn.

item.name: Beast Restraints
entries: Warbeast Gear. Heavy manacles and chains designed to restrain Large or Huge warbeasts. A restrained warbeast can attempt to escape by making a DC 20 Dexterity check or a DC 30 Strength check. The restraints have 25 hit points and an AC of 19.

item.name: Gun Harness
entries: Warbeast Gear. A reinforced frame mounted on a warbeast's back that holds a cannon or similar heavy weapon. Putting on the harness takes 15 minutes; removing it takes 10 minutes. While wearing the harness, a warbeast can use its action to fire a mounted weapon.

item.name: Alchemical Mortar
entries: Warbeast Gear. A mortar mounted on a Gun Harness. It fires Alchemical Mortar Shells in a 10-foot-radius area (Dexterity saving throw for half damage). The mortar has a magazine of 6 shells.

item.name: Adrenaline Enhancement Pump
entries: Enhancement. This mechanikal device is grafted onto a warbeast's adrenal gland. When activated, it floods the creature's system with alchemical stimulants. The warbeast can activate the pump as a bonus action. For the next minute, the warbeast has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks, can use the Dash action as a bonus action, and gains a +2 bonus to its Strength score. When the effect ends, the warbeast must succeed on a DC 16 Constitution saving throw or gain one level of {@condition exhaustion}.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Constitution is permanently reduced by 1d4. The warbeast dies if its Constitution is reduced to 0.

item.name: Aggression Dial
entries: Enhancement. This device is grafted onto a warbeast's skull and modulates its aggression. When activated as a bonus action, it takes 2d6 necrotic damage and becomes compelled to move toward the nearest hostile creature and attack it on each of its turns for 1 minute.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Wisdom is permanently reduced by 1d4. The warbeast dies if its Wisdom is reduced to 0.

item.name: Cerebellum Stimulator
entries: Enhancement. This mechanikal implant stimulates the warbeast's cerebellum, unlocking latent eldritch potential. When activated, the warbeast takes 2d6 lightning damage and can cast the {@spell eldritch blast} cantrip (using the activating warlock's spell attack modifier) until the end of the warlock's next turn.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Intelligence is permanently reduced by 1d4. The warbeast dies if its Intelligence is reduced to 0.

item.name: Epipodial Augmentation
entries: Enhancement. This mechanikal augmentation reinforces a warbeast's limbs, increasing its speed. The warbeast's walking speed increases by 10 feet.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Dexterity is permanently reduced by 1d4. The warbeast dies if its Dexterity is reduced to 0.

item.name: Grafted Weapon
entries: Enhancement. A light steamjack weapon is surgically integrated into the warbeast's body, becoming a natural part of its anatomy. The warbeast gains the weapon as a natural weapon. The cost is the cost of the weapon itself plus any installation fees.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the weapon does not integrate properly. The warbeast takes a permanent penalty of -2 to attack rolls made with the grafted weapon.

item.name: Limbic Spike
entries: Enhancement. A mechanikal spike inserted into the warbeast's limbic system suppresses fear and compulsion responses. The warbeast is immune to the {@condition charmed} and {@condition frightened} conditions.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Charisma is permanently reduced by 1d4. The warbeast dies if its Charisma is reduced to 0.

item.name: Optical Sensors
entries: Enhancement. Mechanikal lenses replace or supplement the warbeast's eyes. If the warbeast does not have darkvision, it gains darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If it already has darkvision, the range of its darkvision increases by 60 feet.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Wisdom (Perception) is permanently reduced by 2.

item.name: Power Plant
entries: Enhancement. A miniaturized steam engine is implanted within the warbeast's torso, providing power for other mechanikal enhancements. A warbeast must have a power plant to use powered enhancements. A power plant requires fuel (coal and water) to operate.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Constitution is permanently reduced by 1d4. The warbeast dies if its Constitution is reduced to 0.

item.name: Respiratory Reservoir
entries: Enhancement. A copper or iron pressure vessel is implanted into the warbeast as a replacement for one of its lungs. Valves on the apparatus slowly introduce additional oxygen into the warbeast's bloodstream. The warbeast can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Constitution is permanently reduced by {@dice 1d4}, and it has disadvantage on saving throws made to avoid exhaustion. The warbeast dies if its Constitution is reduced to 0.

item.name: Secondary Brain
entries: Enhancement. Using mechanikal couplers, a second brain is grafted onto the warbeast's primary brain. If sufficient trauma occurs to the primary brain, the secondary one can take over. The warbeast has advantage on saving throws against being {@condition stunned} or knocked {@condition unconscious}. If the lower of the two rolls would have failed, the warbeast's Intelligence and Charisma scores become 1 until the warbeast finishes a short or long rest.
Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Intelligence is permanently reduced by {@dice 1d4}. The warbeast dies if its Intelligence is reduced to 0.

item.name: Speed Dial
entries: Enhancement. This device is grafted onto the warbeast's chest and connected to its heart. It can be triggered to release a flood of alchemical substances into the warbeast's bloodstream. When activated (as a bonus action by the warbeast's warlock), the warbeast takes {@damage 2d6} necrotic damage. For 1 minute, its walking speed increases by 20 feet, and moving through nonmagical difficult terrain doesn't cost it extra movement.

Botch. If you botch the installation of this enhancement, the warbeast's Constitution is permanently reduced by {@dice 1d4}, and it has disadvantage on saving throws made to avoid exhaustion. The warbeast dies if its Constitution is reduced to 0.

entries.name: Age
entries: Anura age more rapidly than most other folk, reaching maturity in a decade or less. On average, anura live between 50 and 60 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Anura tend toward neutral alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Anura are between 3 and 5 feet tall and weigh between 30 and 60 pounds. Your size is Small or Medium.

entries.name: Amphibious
entries: You can breathe air and water.

entries.name: Poison Glands
entries: Your skin secretes a potent poison. A creature that grapples you or otherwise comes into direct contact with your skin must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or take 1d4 poison damage and become {@condition poisoned} until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Standing Leap
entries: Your long jump is up to 25 feet and your high jump is up to 15 feet, with or without a running start.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak Kworak, which has no written form, and Haavati, and you can speak one other language of your choice, usually the language of your birth kingdom.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: Three main subraces of anura are known in western Immoren: the bullcroak and stickspit anura, and the rarer conjoined soul. Choose one of these subraces.

entries.name: Age
entries: Bog trogs mature quickly and seldom live longer than 65 to 70 years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Bog trogs tend toward neutral and chaotic alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Bog trogs are slightly smaller in size and weight than most average humans. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Amphibious
entries: You can breathe both air and water.

entries.name: Slippery
entries: Your skin has a slick texture that makes you hard to grab. You have advantage on rolls made to escape a grapple.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak Quor-Og, which has no written form, and you can speak one other language of your choice, usually the language of the country or territory your tribe resides in.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: The most common bog trog subraces seen in western Immoren are the angler trog, the saltwater trog, and the wetland trog. Choose one of these subraces.

entries.name: Age
entries: Gatorfolk mature at a faster rate than other species, reaching maturity in 10 to 15 years. On average, gatorfolk live up to 85 years, although some have lived for hundreds of years.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Most gatorfolk are neutral, as they see the world as a harsh place of predators and prey.

entries.name: Size
entries: Gatorfolk are bulkier and taller than humans, often standing nearly 8 feet tall and averaging about 400 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Bite
entries: Your jaws are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal piercing damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier instead of the normal bludgeoning damage for an unarmed strike.

entries.name: Hold Breath
entries: You can hold your breath for 15 minutes.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak Quor-Gar, which has no written form, and you can speak one other language of your choice, usually the language of the country or territory your tribe resides in.

entries.name: Subrace
entries: There are two subraces of gatorfolk common in western Immoren: albinos and ironscale. Choose one of these subraces.

entries.name: Anuran Weapon Training
entries: You have proficiency with halberds, javelins, spears, tridents, and shields.

entries.name: Thundercroak
entries: You can cast the {@spell thunderwave} spell once with this trait, without needing material components, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest. You can also cast the spell using any spell slots you have of 1st level or higher. Wisdom or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell (choose when you select this subrace).

entries.name: Aquatic Camouflage
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill, and you have advantage on any Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks made to hide while in swamps or watery environments.

entries.name: Prehensile Tongue
entries: Your tongue can stretch up to 20 feet. You can use your tongue to manipulate an object, open or close a door or container, or pick up or set down a Tiny object.

entries.name: Wall Crawler
entries: You have advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to climb, and climbing does not cost you extra movement.

entries.name: Dagascar's Touch
entries: You know the {@spell dancing lights}, {@spell guidance}, and {@spell poison spray} cantrips. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for them (choose when you select this subrace). You can cast each of them twice with this trait, without requiring a material component. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Of Two Minds
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, and you have resistance to psychic damage.

entries.name: Expert Crabber
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Survival}) checks made to hunt in swamps and bodies of water.

entries.name: Luminescent Esca
entries: You can make your esca produce bioluminescent light equivalent to the {@spell light} cantrip. Additionally, you can cast the {@spell charm person} spell with this trait, without needing material components. You can cast it a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells (choose when you select this subrace).

entries.name: Deep Sea Diver
entries: You have resistance to cold damage. Additionally, you have advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to swim in treacherous waters.

entries.name: Superior Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fangs
entries: You have oversized fangs that you can use to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, you deal piercing damage equal to {@damage 1d6} + your Strength modifier instead of the typical bludgeoning damage for an unarmed strike.

entries.name: Natural Camouflage
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill, and you have advantage on any Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check made in a jungle, swamp, or forest.

entries.name: Evil Eye
entries: Your training and inner predator are attuned to necromantic energies. You sense each time a necromantic spell is cast within 60 feet of you. If you see the runes of a necromantic spell when it is cast, you learn which spell is being cast.

entries.name: Predatory Sorcery
entries: You know the {@spell chill touch} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it (choose when you select this subrace).

entries.name: Natural Predator
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Survival} skill and your choice of any martial melee weapon that has the heavy or two-handed property. Choose a weapon when you choose this subrace.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: When you aren't wearing armor, your AC equals 13 + your Dexterity modifier. If you are wearing armor that would give you a lower AC, you can use your natural armor to determine your AC instead.

optionalfeature.name: Poisoner (Anura)
entries: Your Constitution score increases by 1, to a maximum of 20.

Your skin's natural poison is more abundant than normal. As a bonus action, you can coat a melee weapon with your poison. A living creature hit by the poisoned weapon takes an extra 1d4 poison damage. Once applied, the poison retains potency for 1 minute before drying. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus (a minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

optionalfeature.name: Death Roller
entries: You have advantage on checks made to initiate or maintain a grapple. While you're grappling a Medium or smaller creature, you can use a bonus action to go prone, pulling the grappled creature with you. If you do so, the grappled creature is {@condition restrained} until it is freed, and if it is underwater, the amount of time it can hold its breath is halved.

optionalfeature.name: Longchops
entries: Your Strength score increases by 1, to a maximum of 20.
Your bite's damage die becomes a d8.

You can attempt to grapple a Medium or smaller creature with your jaws. Until this grapple ends, the target is {@condition restrained}, and you can't bite another target. At the start of each of your turns, you can deal 1d4 piercing damage to one creature you are grappling with your jaws.

spell.name: Absorb Flesh
entries: You reach out to the spirit of a bonded warbeast and break down its body to repair your own. Choose one warbeast that is under your control and in your control range. That warbeast is destroyed, you regain hit points equal to half your hit point maximum, and you gain a number of fury points equal to the warbeast's threshold.

spell.name: Adder's Tongue
entries: Your tongue transforms into a poisonous, serpentine shape you can use to deliver powerful venom. On each of your turns until the spell ends, you can make a melee spell attack with the tongue. If the attack hits, it deals {@damage 1d4} piercing damage and {@damage 1d6} poison damage, and the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or become {@condition poisoned} for 1 minute. You can use this attack a number of times equal to your spellcasting ability modifier (a minimum of once), after which the spell immediately ends.

spell.name: Ashiga's Breath
entries: You unleash a cloud of poisonous ash. Each creature in a 30-foot cone must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 8d8} poison damage and falls unconscious for 1 minute. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and doesn't fall unconscious. A creature that falls unconscious in this way can't be awoken until the effect wears off or it takes damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by {@scaledamage 8d8|5-9|1d8} for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Battle Host
entries: You divert a portion of your power to guide and protect your warbeasts. The speed of each warbeast that is under your control and in your control range increases by 10 feet. Additionally, you gain a +2 bonus to AC as long as you are within 15 feet of a warbeast that is under your control.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the speed of each affected warbeast increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Battle Lust
entries: You call upon primal energies to infuse nearby creatures with a fierce battle lust. Choose up to six creatures within range that you can see. For the duration, each target gains a +2 bonus to attack rolls, is immune to being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, and deals an extra {@damage 1d6} damage when it hits with a melee attack.
At the end of each turn, each target takes {@damage 1d6} psychic damage from the primal energy that fuels this spell.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
